channel.title: Matthew Brensilver Guided Meditations
channel.description: A companion feed of Matthew Brensilver guided meditations, guided practice sessions, and retreat instructions from AudioDharma and Dharma Seed.
channel.itunes:author: Matthew Brensilver
channel.itunes:summary: A companion feed of Matthew Brensilver guided meditations, guided practice sessions, and retreat instructions from AudioDharma and Dharma Seed.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Difficult for the Self to Understand what it can't do
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with simple orientation to body, breath, and a warm attitude of love, then settles into a steady practice of resting attention without overworking it. Matthew Brensilver emphasizes the difference between what we can intentionally do and what belongs to awareness itself, repeatedly returning to the question of “what is your job” and what is not. The talk moves toward a quiet humility: attention can be directed, but awareness and the unfolding of Dharma cannot be forced or owned by the self.
Chapters: 00:11 Arrive in the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25380/?t=11 00:50 A relaxed attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25380/?t=50 02:04 Hope, fear, and re-dedication - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25380/?t=124 03:24 What is your job - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25380/?t=204 07:54 Staying with the step - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25380/?t=474 15:23 The self and the path - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25380/?t=923 16:43 Awareness larger than story - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25380/?t=1003 23:13 Instruction and limitation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25380/?t=1393
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/25380

item.title: Guided Meditation: Awareness is reverential about all phenomena
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on meeting experience without sorting it into pleasant and unpleasant. The practice moves from settling the body, to softening judgment, to recognizing awareness as naturally respectful of whatever arises. He closes by inviting listeners to trust their own wisdom and to speak to themselves as they would guide another meditator.
Chapters: 00:11 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=11 01:13 Feeling Good, Feeling Bad - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=73 02:56 Releasing Preference - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=176 04:17 Just Breathe - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=257 07:01 Reverence for Experience - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=421 10:04 Steady Attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=604 12:24 Effort and Ease - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=744 14:00 Meeting the Mind - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=840 18:59 Listening Within - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25293/?t=1139
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided meditation on opening to this moment by softening obvious tension and the subtler habits of control. The talk moves from surrender and the release of dividing lines, toward a steadier sense of urgency that is not driven by desperation. It closes with a quiet inquiry into awareness, vulnerability, and what can actually be harmed.
Chapters: 00:12 Opening to this moment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25234/?t=12 01:19 Putting down control - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25234/?t=79 05:39 Breath and bracing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25234/?t=339 07:51 Urgency without strain - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25234/?t=471 13:50 Awake, not deliberate - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25234/?t=830 17:48 Awareness and vulnerability - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25234/?t=1068 23:42 What can be harmed - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25234/?t=1422
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/25234

item.title: Guided Meditation: Dedicating our practice
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on arriving, softening, and dedicating practice to the welfare of self and others. The instruction moves from settling body and mind, to letting go of bracing and defensiveness, to reflecting on love, finitude, and the seriousness of practice without strain. The tone stays steady and unforced, with long stretches of repeated phrases that support breathing and attention.
Chapters: 00:30 Arriving Here - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25130/?t=30 02:01 Putting It Down - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25130/?t=121 03:20 Dedicating Practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25130/?t=200 10:29 Love and Finitude - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25130/?t=629 12:16 Quiet Repetition - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25130/?t=736 19:44 Seeing Differently - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25130/?t=1184
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/25130

item.title: Guided Meditation: safe to relax
item.description:
This guided meditation invites a gentle calibration of effort: not too much, not too little, but what this moment asks for. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to breathing in a way that signals safety to the body-mind, so that even amid vulnerability and imperfection it can be okay to relax and let go. Midway through, he brings in a brief Rumi reference to clarify the difference between ordinary rest and the release that comes from stepping out of self-view and possession.
Chapters: 00:12 Calibrating Effort - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25072/?t=12 01:53 Ease in Practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25072/?t=113 04:34 Safety in the Breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25072/?t=274 08:58 Rumi and Rest - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25072/?t=538 13:19 Making No Objects - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25072/?t=799 17:30 Trusting Release - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25072/?t=1050
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/25072

item.title: Guided Meditation: Refuge
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation on refuge, beginning with repeated invitations to settle in Buddha, Dharma, and Sangha, and to let love, gratitude, or reverence soften the heart. The practice gradually widens from the immediate body and breath to a larger sense of being held by the stream of Dharma across time, before ending with a brief reflective closing that points toward the split between subject and object. The tone stays simple and intimate, with long stretches of repetition and spaciousness rather than a lot of discursive explanation.
Chapters: 00:12 Entering Refuge - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25028/?t=12 01:56 Love and Reverence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25028/?t=116 03:46 Settling the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25028/?t=226 06:10 Return to Refuge - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25028/?t=370 09:57 Dharma Across Time - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25028/?t=597 15:05 Less Identified - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25028/?t=905 22:22 Closing Insight - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-25028/?t=1342
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/25028

item.title: Guided Meditation: non-referentiality
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation that gradually shifts from settling with a subtly pleasant sensation into a broader invitation to release reference points altogether. The practice moves through body-based cues, softening the mind’s tendency to measure and orient, and then toward a sense of unpartitioned space, non-referential awareness, and less bracing against pain or time. Near the end, the language becomes more spacious and poetic, with reminders that this moment is innocent and that the trees, as a final image, seem to arrive where they are going.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling with the pleasant - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=12 01:17 A soft smile - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=77 02:38 Dropping the vantage point - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=158 04:35 Renounce orientation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=275 06:19 Fear and love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=379 08:31 Relaxing more deeply - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=511 17:09 Not bracing against pain - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=1029 21:06 The grammar of self - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=1266 25:49 Trees and arrival - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24993/?t=1549
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/24993

item.title: Guided Meditation: The Safety of Letting Go
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that moves from settling the body into a deeper kind of listening, where thought is seen less as a problem than as a protective habit. He gradually reframes thinking, emotion, and even control itself as things that can be met with permission, forgiveness, and mindfulness. The talk ends by opening into the safety that can come from relinquishing control and resting with experience as it is.
Chapters: 00:09 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24838/?t=9 01:29 Unifying the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24838/?t=89 04:23 Deep Listening - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24838/?t=263 08:45 Thinking as Protection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24838/?t=525 12:33 Permission for Feeling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24838/?t=753 15:20 This Moment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24838/?t=920 20:38 Relinquishing Control - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24838/?t=1238
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Resting in Goodness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver opens with a brief, wry set of Melissa Broder quotations that hold dread and humor together, then turns toward a guided meditation on settling and appreciating what is already present. The practice moves from posture and breath to resting with goodness in the body, noticing ordinary life as quietly sufficient, and softening the mind’s habit of organizing around distress. In the closing stretch, he ties enjoyment, letting go, and the Dharma idea of anicca together, suggesting that fear and worry can relax when the mind stops needing to manage everything.
Chapters: 00:19 Comic Prelude - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24760/?t=19 02:21 Finding Posture - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24760/?t=141 05:19 Settling in Goodness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24760/?t=319 09:26 Resting With Breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24760/?t=566 13:06 Being Here - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24760/?t=786 17:17 Enjoyment Deeply - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24760/?t=1037 19:47 Letting Go - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24760/?t=1187 23:27 Fear and Uncertainty - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24760/?t=1407
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Sitting as if already dead
item.description:
This guided meditation uses the image of sitting "as if already dead" to loosen concern, self-protection, and future bargaining, while keeping the mind very awake. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to softening the body, relaxing the heart, and resting in a way that lets love remain when fear and hatred lose their footing. The practice gradually widens into loving-kindness and a simple instruction to slow down, be still, and let the moment do some of the work.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling and relaxing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24705/?t=12 01:00 Softening the heart - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24705/?t=60 02:40 Space from samsara - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24705/?t=160 05:32 Not bartering with future - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24705/?t=332 08:20 Already dead, awake - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24705/?t=500 12:57 Love without fear - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24705/?t=777 19:34 Mettā made vast - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24705/?t=1174 25:25 Hatred is not final - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24705/?t=1525
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/24705

Chapters: 00:12 Settling and relaxing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24705/?t=12 01:00 Softening the heart - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24705/?t=60 02:40 Space from samsara - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24705/?t=160 05:32 Not bartering with future - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24705/?t=332 08:20 Already dead, awake - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24705/?t=500 12:57 Love without fear - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24705/?t=777 19:34 Mettā made vast - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24705/?t=1174 25:25 Hatred is not final - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24705/?t=1525
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Practice: Compassion and Equanimity (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided practice and reflection on compassion and equanimity as two qualities that need to be held in conversation, not treated as a fixed formula. He names compassion as love in the face of suffering and equanimity as love in the face of helplessness, then moves into a brief guided settling, a prayer-like aspiration, and a longer contemplation on grief, mortality, and the limits of what love can change. The talk closes by returning to compassion, with an emphasis on resting without collapse and meeting suffering skillfully.
Chapters: 00:00 Middle path, living balance - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94977/?t=0 01:45 Compassion and equanimity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94977/?t=105 06:04 Settling into practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94977/?t=364 12:02 The trembling heart - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94977/?t=722 13:54 Love and mortality - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94977/?t=834 21:03 Return to compassion - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94977/?t=1263
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/94977/

item.title: Guided Meditation: What Does it Take to Land Here? (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with a brief retreat ritual of surrendering phones and settling into shared silence. Matthew Brensilver then leads a practice centered on letting go safely: meeting fear with awareness, trusting the support of the ground, and allowing the breath to signal ease to the body and mind. In the second half, he shifts to walking meditation, emphasizing simplicity, rhythm, and the felt sense of experience over discursive thinking. The talk closes with practical guidance for where to walk at Spirit Rock and how to adapt the practice if walking is difficult.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening Ceremony - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94973/?t=0 02:20 Settling Into Silence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94973/?t=140 08:31 Letting Go Safely - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94973/?t=511 15:30 Honest Practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94973/?t=930 23:44 Breath and Fear - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94973/?t=1424 34:09 Deepening Into Rest - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94973/?t=2049 49:32 Walking Meditation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94973/?t=2972 57:33 Where to Walk - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-94973/?t=3453

item.title: Guided Metta meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet introduction to metta as a flexible heart practice rather than a feeling to force. He emphasizes that the Dharma does not ask us to pretend, and that loving-kindness may take different forms depending on the moment: compassion, joy, equanimity, forgiveness, gratitude, or simply a simpler phrase that can be held with sincerity. The talk moves from this framing into guided practice, then into questions about bodily constriction, deservingness, grief, and how love and sorrow are intertwined.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening frame - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24617/?t=0 02:07 Finding the right love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24617/?t=127 05:57 Measureless awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24617/?t=357 12:00 Guided metta practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24617/?t=720 40:31 Practice in the world - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24617/?t=2431 50:20 Constriction and worthiness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24617/?t=3020 54:42 Love and grief - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24617/?t=3282 01:00:54 Closing instructions - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-24617/?t=3654
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: entering the dharma realm
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that frames practice as intentionally “entering a Dharma realm,” a field of mind shaped by goodness, kindness, clarity, and letting go. He moves through refuge, the subtle reorientation of perception, and the way this ground helps us meet suffering without adding more to it. The talk closes by turning toward love, vow, and service as expressions of a radical non-violent path.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided meditation on relaxing the body and loosening control so experience can be met more simply. The talk moves from basic instructions to a reflection on equanimity, non-proliferation, and how unpleasant experience can be allowed to end without turning into a story. It closes with a gentler, more embodied image of the moment as something that can soften the body-mind and strengthen the case for love.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Sound and Silence
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on listening as a way of meeting sound, thought, and silence without grasping. The practice moves from simple instruction into a wider field of hearing, including external sounds, internal sounds, equanimity, and a gentle bowing to fear and preoccupation. Near the end, the attention turns toward very distant sounds and the edge where hearing and silence are no longer easy to separate.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Ease Makes Letting Go Safer
item.description:
This guided meditation invites a gentler relationship with agitation by finding a breath that carries ease into the body. Matthew Brensilver emphasizes that awareness does not solve every problem, but it can change how problems are carried, making letting go feel safer when there is some stability and warmth present. The practice moves gradually from settling the body to resting in a subtle sense of care, simplicity, and enoughness.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Activity and Receptivity
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on finding a workable balance between activity and receptivity. Using the image of entering water, he explores how the body gives consent to letting go, how faith and safety shape surrender, and how attention can be directed without becoming rigid. The practice widens into noticing when effort is skillful and when receptivity becomes the steadier ground.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Bright, Steady, Open
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on bright, steady, open awareness: knowing experience without collapsing into the self. He emphasizes practicing with clarity, stability, equanimity, and a gentle relationship to conditioning, then widens the frame toward rest, humility, and spaciousness. The meditation closes by returning to the simple fact of being neither everything nor nothing, but present and responsive.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relax and release
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on relaxing body and mind, with attention to the ways future-thinking, control, and spiritual striving can tighten experience. The practice moves toward letting go more deliberately, including a gentle but sometimes firmer sense of renunciation, and then into refuge and a simple resting with meaning-making set aside. The talk stays close to the felt experience of this moment, returning again and again to stability, clarity, and enoughness.

item.itunes:author: Matthew Brensilver
item.itunes:summary:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on relaxing body and mind, with attention to the ways future-thinking, control, and spiritual striving can tighten experience. The practice moves toward letting go more deliberately, including a gentle but sometimes firmer sense of renunciation, and then into refuge and a simple resting with meaning-making set aside. The talk stays close to the felt experience of this moment, returning again and again to stability, clarity, and enoughness.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Allowing (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This guided meditation invites a very simple release: forgive all phenomena, including the movements of liking, disliking, and selfing. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to not fighting inner conflict, then gradually opens the practice into softening, trusting awareness, and letting the present moment be exactly as it is. In the closing period he offers a strong permission practice—nothing done in these minutes is wrong—before ending with brief retreat announcements.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening forgiveness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93307/?t=0 01:00 Not fighting resistance - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93307/?t=60 10:18 Softening into Dharma - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93307/?t=618 17:26 Settling the spasms - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93307/?t=1046 21:46 No autobiography needed - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93307/?t=1306 26:12 Yielding to awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93307/?t=1572 31:20 Nothing is wrong - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93307/?t=1880 39:14 Closing announcements - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93307/?t=2354
Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relax a Little Further (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided retreat meditation built around one repeated instruction: relax a little further. He links that softening to letting go of assumptions about self, progress, and control, and to recognizing practice as a balance between effort and receptivity. As the meditation deepens, he turns toward attention, pain, and refuge, ending with a quiet reminder that simply being alive is enough.
Chapters: 00:00 Relax further - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93303/?t=0 05:29 Doing and surrender - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93303/?t=329 09:57 Attitude and renunciation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93303/?t=597 16:12 Attention opens - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93303/?t=972 18:17 Care for pain - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93303/?t=1097 24:28 Receiving what is here - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93303/?t=1468 30:44 Love without preference - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93303/?t=1844 38:19 Refuge everywhere - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93303/?t=2299 44:08 Retreat notes and close - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93303/?t=2648

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relaxing all clinging
item.description:
This guided meditation explores relaxation as a form of surrender rather than mere physical softening. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to letting go of control, stepping out of narrow identifications, and allowing the mind to rest without feeding familiar grooves of worry and selfing. The practice moves toward tenderness, forgiveness, and trust in the goodness already present, ending in a quieter sense of awareness less dominated by self.

item.title: Guided Meditation: How distant is freedom?
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by turning attention toward a felt sense of freedom and asking how distance itself is created by the mind. Matthew Brensilver invites a gentle, less controlling relationship to breath and body, then widens into lineage, trust, and the recognition that mindfulness is not another project of self-management. The talk settles into a quiet instruction to rest in the present moment without keeping constant tabs on what is right or wrong.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Internal and External Sound and Silence
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on listening: to external sound, internal sound, and the silence around and within them. The practice moves gently from noticing the field of hearing to softening identification with thinking, then toward receptivity, non-preference, and a quieter sense of presence. He also draws on a Shakespeare line, a brief Mother Teresa story, and Martin Luther King Jr. to underline how listening can be both contemplative and ethical.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Forgiving Body, Mind and World
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a gentle guided meditation built around forgiveness as a practical loosening of friction rather than a forced state of peace. The practice moves from sensing what the mind needs, through body-based softening and truce with pain, toward a spacious awareness that can hold reactions, fear, hope, and ordinary sounds without struggle. It ends with a quiet inquiry into what awareness remains even when control drops away.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Urgency of Feeling
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on the "urgency" that can arise from feeling. The practice begins with settling the body and breathing, then turns toward noticing hidden agendas, control strategies, and the way emotional surges can seem like commands. As the meditation deepens, he repeatedly returns to relaxing, absorbing feeling, and letting breathing become safe again.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relaxing and Letting Feeling be Feeling
item.description:
This guided meditation invites a simple, careful relationship to pleasant and unpleasant feeling: know it as it is, and notice what softens when it no longer has to mean something about you. The instruction moves from relaxing obvious tension into a narrower and then wider field of attention, including breath, body, and difficult experience with a light touch of kindness. It ends with a quiet emphasis on not bracing against experience and on the felt necessity of love.
Chapters: 00:01 Know Feeling Simply - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93046/?t=1 01:22 Relax the Urgency - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93046/?t=82 05:24 Soften the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93046/?t=324 08:53 Feel the Breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93046/?t=533 14:16 Uncontrived Breathing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93046/?t=856 18:06 Subtle In-Breath and Out - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93046/?t=1086 21:25 Widen the Aperture - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93046/?t=1285 28:35 Radical Inclusion - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93046/?t=1715 33:50 Necessity of Love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-93046/?t=2030

item.title: Guided Meditation: moving towards compassion
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation on how compassion can grow from directly meeting suffering rather than turning away from it. He begins with an Issa poem about dukkha and then repeatedly returns to simple supports: settling the body, soothing the heart, and allowing mindfulness to include sorrow and happiness. The practice gradually widens into a steadier wish for less suffering, with imagery, silent phrases, and a sense of protecting love as it meets pain.

item.title: Guided Meditation: don't wait to let go
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with posture, settling, and a simple bodily yielding to gravity and breath. Matthew then turns toward impermanence, sufficiency, and the instruction not to delay letting go, before opening into a brief reflection on loving-kindness and the widening that can come when there is more space around grief. The final minutes include a personal remembrance of Kathleen Haviland, which grounds the talk in loss, relationship, and the practical compassion of practice.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=0 02:41 Yielding to gravity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=161 06:23 Finitude and enough - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=383 12:16 Do not wait - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=736 13:08 Resting after surrender - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=788 22:25 Practice loving - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=1345 23:44 More space around pain - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=1424 25:59 Remembering Kathleen - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23721/?t=1559
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Abiding in Care
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on care as a quiet, stabilizing quality of mind and body. The practice moves from settling posture and softening tension into a broader sense of kindness, non-contention, and heartfulness that does not need to perform or fix anything. He then connects love with breath, grief, and the full range of feeling, suggesting that compassion can make pain more bearable without reducing its poignancy.
Chapters: 00:27 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23687/?t=27 01:43 Softening Tension - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23687/?t=103 03:44 The Heart's Realm - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23687/?t=224 06:17 No Need to Perform - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23687/?t=377 08:47 Finding a Support - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23687/?t=527 11:57 Love and Spaciousness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23687/?t=717 16:51 Four Forms of Love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23687/?t=1011 19:37 Breath and Presence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23687/?t=1177 28:01 Grief and Poignancy - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23687/?t=1681

item.title: Guided Meditation: Renunciation and Dana
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that moves from settling the body into a practical reflection on renunciation: laying down greed, distress, and the anxious project of managing life. He then links letting go with generosity, saying that when the grip softens, life is experienced as gift and the breath, body, and remaining life are all forms of dāna. The practice stays grounded and intimate, emphasizing relaxation, warmth, and the release of ownership and defensiveness rather than suppression.
Chapters: 00:11 Opening and theme - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23673/?t=11 01:53 What to put down - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23673/?t=113 04:20 Unclenching the fist - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23673/?t=260 06:45 Greed and distress - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23673/?t=405 09:39 Letting control soften - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23673/?t=579 15:16 Ownership and gratitude - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23673/?t=916 18:25 Breath as dāna - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23673/?t=1105 22:57 Life becomes gift - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23673/?t=1377
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Everything is Passing
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation centered on impermanence, beginning with settling, breath, and a gentle invocation of the whole history of practice. He gradually widens the frame to include reverence, loving-kindness, and forgiveness of conditioning, then turns to a clear reflection on anicca: not just that things pass, but that everything is always passing, including the sense of the observer. The practice closes by softening into the felt experience of breathing and the changing nature of awareness itself.
Chapters: 00:00 Arriving and settling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23662/?t=0 02:00 Calling in practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23662/?t=120 04:30 Reverence and devotion - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23662/?t=270 07:25 Loving-kindness and care - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23662/?t=445 10:23 Understanding wandering - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23662/?t=623 14:28 Forgiving conditioning - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23662/?t=868 18:17 The flow of anicca - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23662/?t=1097 25:06 Melting into breathing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23662/?t=1506
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Emphasizing Tranquility
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on tranquility as something that settles when we stop adding effort and willfulness. He distinguishes between times when the mind is tired and needs nurturing brightness, and times when it is alert enough to rest in quiet, subtle sensations, silence, and ease. The talk gradually turns toward release, forgiveness, and a simpler sense of sufficiency, ending with a few bells and a period of settling.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23669/?t=0 00:30 Ceasing to Strive - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23669/?t=30 01:29 Yielding to Silence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23669/?t=89 02:49 Finding the Quiet Beneath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23669/?t=169 05:30 Brightness Or Rest - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23669/?t=330 07:26 Listening to Tranquility - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23669/?t=446 10:51 Signs of Calm - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23669/?t=651 15:01 Putting Down Burdens - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23669/?t=901 18:14 Enoughness and Closure - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23669/?t=1094

item.title: Guided Meditation Presence is not a Transaction
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation centered on settling the body, softening effort, and letting the breath signal that the moment is safe enough to relax. The practice turns toward receiving experience without trying to extract anything from it, contrasting transactional habits of greed and aversion with a more receptive trust. He closes by broadening the frame: meditation is not something placed beside life, but a way of letting life rest within the Dharma.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Clinging is complicated, letting go simplifies
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quietly structured guided meditation on how clinging complicates experience and letting go can simplify it. He repeatedly invites the listener to rest in the body, notice hope, fear, and thinking without finishing their stories, and soften into the breath. The practice gradually shifts toward gentleness and warmth, with an emphasis on meeting the mind’s movements without neglecting what matters.

item.title: Guided meditation in Style of Shinzen Young: Mental Images, Auditory Thinking and Emotion in the Body
item.description:
This guided meditation works through Matthew Brensilver’s Shinzen-style framing of experience as seeing, hearing, and feeling. It begins with settling the body, then turns toward internal visual images, auditory thinking, and emotional sensations, showing how each can be known without becoming identified with it. In the final stretch, the practice broadens to the whole triad and ends with a gentle investigation of how suffering assembles beneath awareness.

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided meditation that begins with warmth and goodwill, then narrows attention to a simple resting place in the body, breath, and sound. He returns again and again to the ordinariness of goodness, trust, and ease, suggesting that practice can make us less intimidated by the future and less burdened by the past. Near the end, he turns toward the relation between care and rest, pointing to the need for cycles of devotion and recovery rather than constant strain.

item.itunes:author: Matthew Brensilver
item.itunes:summary:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided meditation that begins with warmth and goodwill, then narrows attention to a simple resting place in the body, breath, and sound. He returns again and again to the ordinariness of goodness, trust, and ease, suggesting that practice can make us less intimidated by the future and less burdened by the past. Near the end, he turns toward the relation between care and rest, pointing to the need for cycles of devotion and recovery rather than constant strain.

item.title: Guided Meditation: If you're looking, you're lost
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by inviting a felt sense of inspiration and a dharma longing that is different from craving to get somewhere. From there, Matthew Brensilver repeatedly points toward attuning to what serves, and toward the simple recognition that awakening is already available in ordinary experience. The instruction then turns to relinquishing preference, loosening willfulness, and resting in awareness without trying to manage it.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Letting go of limitation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that moves from settling and grounding into a gentle inquiry into how ideas of limitation constrict the mind. He invites listeners to soften self-protective habits, feel safe enough to rest in breath and body, and notice how spaciousness can arise without effort. The talk later widens into equanimity and a quiet recognition that, when experience is no longer so compelling, it becomes more natural to lay burdens down.
Chapters: 00:12 Grounding and Settling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23459/?t=12 02:42 Putting Down Limitation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23459/?t=162 05:31 Clinging and Vastness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23459/?t=331 09:39 Safety in the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23459/?t=579 15:32 No Preference - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23459/?t=932 16:12 Nothing to Manufacture - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23459/?t=972 21:23 Samsara and Weariness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23459/?t=1283 26:14 Closing Blessing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23459/?t=1574
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Grounding and Compassion
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by easing attention into silence and sound, then treats thoughts as just more sound rather than something to cling to. From there, Matthew Brensilver moves into settling, using a Thich Nhat Hanh image of nectar sinking in a glass, and then turns toward compassion by reflecting on suffering, precariousness, and gratitude. The practice becomes more intimate as he invites listeners to bring to mind someone who is suffering, feel the weight of their pain, and let that meeting soften into warmth and care.
Chapters: 00:12 Entering Silence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23421/?t=12 01:29 Thoughts as Sound - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23421/?t=89 04:44 Settling Like Nectar - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23421/?t=284 10:03 Suffering and Gratitude - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23421/?t=603 13:45 Bringing Someone to Mind - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23421/?t=825 16:43 Taking It In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23421/?t=1003 18:51 Love in Response - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23421/?t=1131 20:50 Softening Into Compassion - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23421/?t=1250
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Forgive all phenomena
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a gentle guided meditation that begins with settling the body and taking up a careful, relaxed posture. He then turns to the felt mechanics of craving and the habit of always reaching for the next thing, before shifting into the central instruction to “forgive all phenomena” — including pleasantness and pain — and trust the quieting that follows. The talk closes with a more spacious, effortless resting and a few bell tones.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Meditating on June 19
item.description:
This guided meditation moves gently between breath, body, and awareness, then opens into a quiet reflection on love, freedom, and nonviolence. Matthew Brensilver names Juneteenth and uses it to frame the practice as staying close to love, feeling the intensity of being human, and sensing the innocence of the wish for peace. Later, he points to the image of peace at every sense door—body, eye, ear, and mind—as a way of understanding awakening as a soft, loving openness.
Chapters: 02:30 Finding the Anchor - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23239/?t=150 04:42 Staying with Awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23239/?t=282 07:47 Juneteenth and Freedom - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23239/?t=467 12:13 Peace at the Senses - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23239/?t=733 15:53 Stopping the Fight - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23239/?t=953 17:19 Settling in Silence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23239/?t=1039
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Following Interest
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with a Thich Nhat Hanh image of settling, then turns toward letting experience rise rather than forcing it down. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to the ease of not having to manage attention, inviting trust that practice can happen without strain or performance. The guidance then shifts into following what is genuinely interesting, treating curiosity as a kind of receptive, non-extractive attention.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling Like Nectar - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23189/?t=12 01:01 Letting Things Surface - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23189/?t=61 04:34 Trusting Non-Doing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23189/?t=274 08:41 Following Interest - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23189/?t=521 09:29 Curiosity as Practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23189/?t=569 15:15 Receptive Attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23189/?t=915 17:04 Counting and Closing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23189/?t=1024
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/23189

item.title: Guided Meditation: Embodiment and Allowing
item.description:
This guided meditation invites a quiet shift from efforting to willingness, using the breath and body as a way to become more available to experience. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to themes of embodiment, softening, and allowing, especially when meditation feels difficult or impossible. The practice builds toward a spacious view of awareness that can hold pain, aversion, and uncertainty without tightening around them.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Fresh Moment
item.description:
In this guided meditation, Matthew Brensilver invites a quiet shift from expecting the next moment to meeting experience freshly. He frames practice around not being startled by impermanence, relaxing what can soften, and letting the breath hold the body with a steady, soothing presence. Later, he turns toward curiosity, not-knowing, and the release of self-repetition as ways to stay awake and alive to what is here.
Chapters: 00:12 Arriving and settling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23004/?t=12 00:32 Not startled by change - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23004/?t=32 03:08 Breath as soothing support - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23004/?t=188 06:26 Allowing the present moment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23004/?t=386 12:12 Following genuine interest - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23004/?t=732 17:17 The aliveness of not knowing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23004/?t=1037 21:06 Releasing expectation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23004/?t=1266 21:46 Quiet and selfing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-23004/?t=1306
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/23004

item.title: Guided Meditation: dropping secret agendas (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on loosening hidden striving: the wish to find, fix, control, or curate experience. He moves from relaxing the body and letting the breath, sounds, and awareness arrive on their own, toward a quieter look at how grasping and aversion shape attention and the sense of self. In the final section he turns to walking meditation as a way to carry steadiness, release, and beginning again into daily movement.
Chapters: 00:01 Settle and relax - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90259/?t=1 02:28 Hidden agendas - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90259/?t=148 05:57 Wanting and attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90259/?t=357 10:48 Let the self loosen - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90259/?t=648 18:12 Surrendering words - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90259/?t=1092 19:22 Practice in the hall - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90259/?t=1162 39:47 First and second arrows - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90259/?t=2387 42:21 Walking as training - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90259/?t=2541
Source: Dharma Seed

He moves from relaxing the body and letting the breath, sounds, and awareness arrive on their own, toward a quieter look at how grasping and aversion shape attention and the sense of self. In the final section he turns to walking meditation as a way to carry steadiness, release, and beginning again into daily movement.

item.title: Guided Metta Meditation - Metta for All Beings (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver frames metta as one of the Brahma-viharas, or “four species of love,” then shows how loving-kindness can function as cultivation, concentration, and purification. The talk moves from the way metta loosens self-view to a guided practice that begins with the body and phrases, then opens outward to all beings. It ends by holding the sincerity of the wish alongside the fact that suffering is vast and unresolved.
Chapters: 00:00 Four kinds of love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=0 01:57 Love changes self-view - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=117 05:08 Measureless and quiet - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=308 06:23 Cultivation and purification - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=383 11:48 Settling into practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=708 22:44 Phrases in the present - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=1364 26:46 Resting in kindness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=1606 31:13 Love in hard places - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=1873 33:15 All beings at ease - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-90257/?t=1995

item.title: Guided Meditation: Non-referentiality
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver begins with a light, self-aware basketball anecdote and then turns quickly into a guided meditation on settling, relaxing, and sensing the body as light and gravity. The main teaching explores how the mind locates itself through reference points—body, preference, craving, aversion—and invites a period of practice “without referentiality.” The closing section softens into endings, dissolution, and a Rumi line about living in the nowhere you come from, returning to spaciousness without fixed self-reference.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Medtiation: Checking Hindrances, Checking Defilements
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with settling into the body and then scans the five hindrances as a practical pulse check: restlessness, dullness, craving, aversion, and doubt. The emphasis stays gentle and observational, with repeated invitations to notice what is present without forcing anything away. As the practice unfolds, the talk turns toward relaxing the effort to control experience and allowing attention to become more open, unmixed, and receptive.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Softening the Self
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on softening the felt sense of self through relaxation, safety, and widening awareness. The practice begins with posture and settling, then turns toward the strain of clinging and willfulness, suggesting that letting go reveals a quieter brightness already present. In the later stages, he explores the self as a knot of tension and invites a more porous, fluid way of being, with an emphasis on anicca and the way identity can loosen when the mind feels safe.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Arousal and Valence Signifying Nothing
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on working directly with arousal and valence: how intensity, pleasantness, and unpleasantness are sensed in the body, and how they can be held without turning immediately into stories. The practice emphasizes settling with breath, widening the field of experience, and allowing feeling to remain vivid without making it mean too much. As the talk develops, the instruction turns toward decoupling emotion from elaboration so that experience can be present without collapsing into identity or self-judgment.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Surrendering the Territory of Me
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with simple sensory attention and gradually widens into a softer relationship with body, sound, and awareness. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to the felt sense of self as a territory we patrol, then invites a gentler letting go of that guarding and a more intimate experience of spaciousness. The closing instructions emphasize non-preference: meeting contraction and ease alike without making them a problem.

item.title: Guided Meditation: The Pull of Time
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation that moves from relaxing tension in the body into a broader reflection on care, attention, and the limits of control. The practice repeatedly returns to kindness toward pain and tightness, then widens into the ways wanting and fear shape attention and keep us oriented toward a future that never fully arrives. In the closing arc, he points to letting go, receiving the present moment, and recognizing that insight into the Four Noble Truths can be available right now.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling and Relaxing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22694/?t=12 01:37 Tending the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22694/?t=97 03:06 Care and Lineage - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22694/?t=186 05:20 Practice and Human Longing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22694/?t=320 09:48 Wanting and Fear - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22694/?t=588 15:16 Letting Go to Receive - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22694/?t=916 21:02 Insight on Offer - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22694/?t=1262 26:36 Not Missing It - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22694/?t=1596

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relax and Let go
item.description:
This guided meditation settles first into posture and deeper breathing, then moves toward a quieter willingness to stop trying to solve life. Matthew Brensilver emphasizes surrender, rest, and receiving the breath more fully, with a brief image from Thich Nhat Hanh and a nod to the Three Refuges as sources of soothing. The latter part becomes a long counting practice, steadying attention through repetition and simplicity.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relaxing the tension called 'me'
item.description:
This guided meditation invites a softening around the felt tension of selfing, moving from settling the body into a careful inquiry into how control is maintained through feeling, thought, and image. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to surrender as a simple laying down of strategy, and toward the end he emphasizes relaxing the contraction that accompanies uncertainty. The practice stays quiet and direct, with the instruction to notice what can relax and to allow the rest to remain.
Chapters: 00:13 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22669/?t=13 01:28 Touching Tranquility - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22669/?t=88 04:01 Relax What Can - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22669/?t=241 05:24 Self as Headquarters - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22669/?t=324 09:38 Seeing the Mechanics - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22669/?t=578 15:16 From Control to Wakefulness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22669/?t=916 23:44 Contraction and Uncertainty - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22669/?t=1424 25:00 Habitualizing Impermanence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22669/?t=1500
Source: AudioDharma

item.itunes:summary:
This guided meditation invites a softening around the felt tension of selfing, moving from settling the body into a careful inquiry into how control is maintained through feeling, thought, and image. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to surrender as a simple laying down of strategy, and toward the end he emphasizes relaxing the contraction that accompanies uncertainty. The practice stays quiet and direct, with the instruction to notice what can relax and to allow the rest to remain.
Chapters: 00:13 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22669/?t=13 01:28 Touching Tranquility - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22669/?t=88 04:01 Relax What Can - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22669/?t=241 05:24 Self as Headquarters - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22669/?t=324 09:38 Seeing the Mechanics - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22669/?t=578 15:16 From Control to Wakefulness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22669/?t=916 23:44 Contraction and Uncertainty - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22669/?t=1424 25:00 Habitualizing Impermanence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22669/?t=1500
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/22669

item.title: Guided Meditation: Stillness and the Dissolution of the Predictive Mind
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with settling the body and gradually shifts toward the breath as a simple object of awareness. He links relaxation to a softer relationship with dukkha, then moves into a teaching on how meditation loosens the mind’s habit of predicting the next moment and imagining continuity. The talk closes by pointing toward samadhi as a quiet dissolution of that predictive architecture and a more direct falling into the present.
Chapters: 00:01 Opening and settling in - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22663/?t=1 01:31 Doing much less - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22663/?t=91 02:48 Softening the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22663/?t=168 07:06 Grounding and openness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22663/?t=426 10:38 Breath and aliveness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22663/?t=638 16:48 Predicting the future - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22663/?t=1008 19:46 Samadhi and craving - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22663/?t=1186 23:38 Falling into presence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22663/?t=1418
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Much Becomes Incidental
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation centered on listening to silence and letting the usual mental inventory become incidental. The practice repeatedly returns to receiving the moment without project-making, self-management, or the need to solve experience. As the talk unfolds, surrender is framed not as passivity but as a temporary release of control in which even difficulty can be held more lightly.
Chapters: 00:13 Listening to silence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22668/?t=13 01:58 What becomes incidental - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22668/?t=118 03:43 Receiving the moment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22668/?t=223 06:41 No projects, no building - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22668/?t=401 08:29 Surrender and love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22668/?t=509 09:27 Receiving space - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22668/?t=567 16:54 A new chance to let go - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22668/?t=1014 20:18 Emerging unchanged - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22668/?t=1218
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Reverence & Renunciation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that frames practice as both reverence and renunciation. He invites a posture of sanctity without strain, then guides attention to the breath as a way of putting down multitasking, worry, story-making, and clinging. The talk widens into a quiet reflection on lineage, sincerity, and the way letting go can simplify experience and make breathing more intimate.

item.title: Guided Meditation: All Phenomena are Friendly
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation that steadily loosens the pressure of self-judgment and future-minded striving. He invites listeners to set down the habit of making the self into an object, rest in the breath, and become less intimidated by uncertainty. The practice gradually opens into a quieter Dharma perspective: experience can be met more softly, even when it hurts.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Arriving and Opening
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a gentle guided meditation centered on arriving, softening self-conscious effort, and letting the body be as it is. The practice moves from settling posture and attention, to checking for places that need patience or care, to resting with the breath and the felt sense of the body. He closes by framing the practice in terms of relinquishment, true nourishment, and a quiet metta-like response to agitation.
Chapters: 00:12 Arriving Gently - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22607/?t=12 01:52 Sensing What Needs Care - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22607/?t=112 05:02 Relaxing Self-Image - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22607/?t=302 07:55 Breathing With Safety - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22607/?t=475 12:45 Relinquishment and Nourishment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22607/?t=765 13:53 Extended Silent Practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22607/?t=833 20:17 Meeting Agitation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22607/?t=1217
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Mostly Quiet Sit
item.description:
This mostly quiet guided sit begins with a simple invitation to stop fighting awareness and rest with what cannot be stopped. It then widens into several possible anchors—breath, body, soundscape, feeling tone, and even auditory thinking—while repeatedly softening the sense of effort and self-management. In the last portion, Matthew turns toward a poetic line about a burden that remains heavy even when understood as empty, framing practice as careful attention and love rather than dismissal.
Chapters: 00:00 Rest with awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22625/?t=0 04:48 Choose an anchor - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22625/?t=288 06:54 Ease the selfing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22625/?t=414 09:56 Soften, widen, relax - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22625/?t=596 24:42 Include inner sound - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22625/?t=1482 30:09 Heavy becomes light - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22625/?t=1809
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Life without Words
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation centered on the question of what life feels like without words. The practice moves from noticing the mind’s urge to narrativize experience, through working with urgency and craving, into a long stretch of silence and a gradual turn toward stillness. Near the end, he explicitly frames the meditation in terms of relinquishing ownership and self-making, invoking the Buddha’s instruction to give up what is not yours.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Being Held, Settling In
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with the felt sense of being held by the world, using rain, earth, and bodily support as doorways into settling. The practice moves through breath awareness, working with urgency and false alarms, and then broadens into noticing suffering more clearly without being overwhelmed by it. In the second half, he introduces walking meditation, alternating anchors like seeing, hearing, feet, whole body, and loving-kindness, and closes by emphasizing freshness, renunciation, and trust in the Dharma.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Peace is Close
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation centered on settling the body, setting worries aside without force, and softening into immediate experience. The practice moves from simple breathing to a felt sense of defenselessness, then to noticing how the mind elaborates and constructs a self. In the later portion, the teaching opens into a subtle contemplation of being unseen, empty, and no longer held together by familiar identity.
Chapters: 00:14 Arriving Together - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22488/?t=14 00:51 Landing In Breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22488/?t=51 02:45 Putting Worries Down - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22488/?t=165 05:06 Offering The Heart - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22488/?t=306 07:04 Breathing Without Elaboration - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22488/?t=424 12:07 Small Shifts Matter - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22488/?t=727 17:59 Constructing Self - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22488/?t=1079 22:07 Unseen And Empty - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22488/?t=1327
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Sangha & Self
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on Sangha and self, beginning with a brief reflection on why community matters for Dharma practice. He draws on Agnes Callard’s Open Socrates to frame wisdom as something we arrive at with others, then leads the group into a meditation on mutual dependence, self-view, and the subtle ways identity organizes experience. The practice opens toward noticing how the sense of “I” is built, and how that can soften when seen clearly.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening and context - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22405/?t=0 03:19 Open Socrates - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22405/?t=199 05:53 The jewel of Sangha - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22405/?t=353 06:58 Mutual dependence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22405/?t=418 10:44 Seeing self-view - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22405/?t=644 19:46 Noticing the vantage point - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22405/?t=1186 24:25 Subtle images and words - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22405/?t=1465
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Abiding in Goodness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on abiding in goodness, beginning with the Dalai Lama’s claim that kindness and compassion are necessities rather than luxuries. The practice moves from recollecting love as nourishment, to sensing the ethical atmosphere of sangha, to widening into the shared wish of all beings to be well. Later he brings in the Buddha and Joan Halifax to connect self-care, non-harming, and the embodied balance of a "strong back, soft front."
Chapters: 00:12 Love as Necessity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22332/?t=12 06:17 Abiding in Love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22332/?t=377 12:22 Self and Others - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22332/?t=742 18:29 Grief and Courage - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22332/?t=1109 23:03 Deep Faith in Love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22332/?t=1383 26:15 Closing Refrain - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22332/?t=1575
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/22332

item.title: Guided Meditation: Freeing Experience
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation built around a simple reversal: freedom is not getting a self to win, but loosening the tendency to own experience. He begins with posture and breath, then gradually widens the field to include sensations, feelings, thoughts, and the sense of being a protagonist. The practice settles into a quieter intimacy with awareness, where experience can arise and pass without being seized.
Chapters: 00:25 Freedom Reframed - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22215/?t=25 01:20 Settling the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22215/?t=80 03:23 Including What Appears - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22215/?t=203 08:37 Freeing Experience - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22215/?t=517 13:45 Less Possession - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22215/?t=825 15:39 Impersonal Arising - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22215/?t=939 24:28 Equanimity and Control - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22215/?t=1468 25:57 Resting in Awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22215/?t=1557
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/22215

item.title: Guided Meditation: Resting in Goodness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on resting in goodness rather than bracing against experience. He emphasizes letting ordinary goodness soothe agitation, forgiving the mind at a deep level, and trusting awareness and love to carry the practice. The talk gradually widens from settling posture to a softer relationship with thought, clinging, and whatever can be enjoyed in the present field of experience.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22137/?t=12 01:05 Goodness as Soothe - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22137/?t=65 03:14 Attention and Agitation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22137/?t=194 05:50 Pressure on Self - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22137/?t=350 08:19 Meditation by Itself - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22137/?t=499 13:46 Clinging and Aliveness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22137/?t=826 25:05 Enjoy What Can Be - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22137/?t=1505
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/22137

item.title: Guided Meditation: Grounding and Opening
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a grounded guided meditation centered on settling into the body, especially the belly, bones, and sense of earthiness. He repeatedly returns to the Buddha’s earth-touching gesture as a way to move attention out of thought and into direct bodily presence. As the practice unfolds, he opens into noticing affect and emotional activation with patience, then loosens ideas about how meditation or life should be, emphasizing simple aliveness over performance.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Putting Down the World
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on what it means to “put down the world” without turning away from it. The practice moves from settling the body and mind, to resting without bartering for outcomes, to meeting experience directly through breath, sound, and sensation. In the final movement, he returns to the paradox that letting go can deepen love, care, and a more workable response to life.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Breathing into Compassion
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with James Baldwin’s words on pain, connection, and the possibility of release through shared suffering. The practice then moves into sensing the body as a whole, settling attention, and using the breath to soften identification and open compassion outward. Near the end, he widens the frame to all beings and closes with a sense of how deeply lives intertwine.
Chapters: 00:25 Baldwin and pain - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22016/?t=25 03:38 Sense the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22016/?t=218 06:46 Breath across the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22016/?t=406 09:53 Simple resting - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22016/?t=593 13:38 Pain as connection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22016/?t=818 16:12 Compassion opens wider - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22016/?t=972 22:50 Breath as love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22016/?t=1370 26:58 Shared life and closing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-22016/?t=1618

item.title: Guided meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with settling the body and noticing what feels pleasant in stillness. From there, he develops equanimity as a way to stop trying to rearrange experience, opening attention more broadly and softening the weight we give to thoughts, concerns, and even personal identity. The practice closes by emphasizing spaciousness, a lighter touch with the stream of experience, and the simple instruction to keep opening.
Chapters: 00:29 Settling the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21934/?t=29 01:17 Finding what feels good - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21934/?t=77 02:54 Less urgency, more equanimity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21934/?t=174 07:18 Breath as refuge - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21934/?t=438 08:12 Mindfulness and letting go - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21934/?t=492 11:56 Offering the heart up - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21934/?t=716 17:00 Widening attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21934/?t=1020 24:03 Lightness and space - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21934/?t=1443 27:50 Keep opening - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21934/?t=1670
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Mostly Quiet Sit
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with a brief welcome and then settles into a mostly quiet sit, with only occasional instruction. Matthew emphasizes sensing where attention feels appropriate, letting the Dharma become personal rather than merely borrowed from tradition. The sit moves through softening, support from sangha, and a spacious acceptance of present conditions, ending with a quieter closing bell sequence.
Chapters: 01:24 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21888/?t=84 03:34 Personalizing Practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21888/?t=214 09:39 Letting Go - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21888/?t=579 16:08 Sangha Support - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21888/?t=968 21:44 Radical Permission - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21888/?t=1304 27:17 Closing Bells - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21888/?t=1637
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Self-acceptance
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on self-acceptance that moves from settling the body into a broad, compassionate view of conditioning, history, and the forces that shape a life. He invites a radical yes to experience—not as a way to become better, but as the ground from which change can actually happen. The practice ends in a simple self-directed loving-kindness sequence: may I be happy, safe, protected, and at peace.
Chapters: 01:52 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21873/?t=112 04:04 Body and Awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21873/?t=244 06:23 Kindness for Conditioning - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21873/?t=383 09:12 Accepting My Life - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21873/?t=552 15:22 A Radical Yes - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21873/?t=922 17:59 End the Inner Game - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21873/?t=1079 26:23 Loving-Kindness Closing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21873/?t=1583
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21873

item.title: Guided meditation: Affect and the Lure of Thought
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation on meeting affect directly rather than using thought to manage discomfort. He contrasts a gentle settling into practice with the more decisive attitude of “starting on a dime,” then repeatedly returns to renunciation as a willingness to feel what is present. The meditation emphasizes body-based attention, equanimity with emotional activation, and a quiet release of urgency and self-concern.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling into practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21869/?t=12 00:40 Start on a dime - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21869/?t=40 01:43 Putting thoughts down - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21869/?t=103 04:30 Renunciation as feeling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21869/?t=270 06:36 Body and emotion - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21869/?t=396 15:51 Equanimity and urgency - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21869/?t=951 17:54 This is your life - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21869/?t=1074 23:36 Release of self - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21869/?t=1416
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Attention and Love
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation that begins with settling the body, moving attention through chest, belly, breath, and earthiness. The practice gradually opens into a reflection on how attention reveals love and care, then turns toward the difficulty of hatred and the choice to meet it with breathing and affection. The close is sparse and intimate, with repeated pointers to simply feel love and allow it to be known.

item.title: Guided Meditation: What can you stop doing?
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores loving-kindness as something different from ordinary wanting: not a stronger preference, but a letting go of clinging, fear, and motivational desperation. He moves from Buddhist teaching to the felt realities of attachment in family and romantic love, then toward a more honest, less possessive way of caring that can include grief and dukkha without becoming overwhelmed by them. The talk closes by linking metta to moral elevation, rest, and a heart made less available to hatred.
Chapters: 00:16 Opening into metta - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21856/?t=16 00:35 Love beyond wanting - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21856/?t=35 01:49 Wanting and fear - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21856/?t=109 03:29 Metta as release - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21856/?t=209 04:26 Approaching what hurts - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21856/?t=266 06:08 Kindness, not niceness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21856/?t=368 08:32 Attachment with grace - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21856/?t=512 10:20 Love without control - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21856/?t=620 12:22 Abiding in goodness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21856/?t=742
