item.title: Guided Meditation: Putting Down the Project of Your Life
item.description:
This guided meditation invites a gentle settling into posture, breath, and a quieter relationship to vigilance. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to the sense that worry, defensiveness, and the “project called my life” can be set down for a moment, making room for tranquility and not knowing. The practice gradually opens into the felt relief of letting go and the capacities that arise with a calmer nervous system: love, kindness, compassion, forgiveness, care, and peace.
Chapters: 00:12 Arriving and settling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21862/?t=12 02:45 Relaxed, alert posture - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21862/?t=165 05:13 Patience with worry - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21862/?t=313 08:05 Breath over vigilance - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21862/?t=485 11:34 Tranquility and not knowing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21862/?t=694 15:26 Putting down the project - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21862/?t=926 17:18 Returning without carrying - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21862/?t=1038 24:15 Wholesome qualities emerge - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21862/?t=1455

item.title: Guided Meditation: It's all come to this
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation centered on gathering the goodness of practice and meeting the present moment without violence. The talk moves from recollecting years of practice and the pain and wisdom that have led here, into a gentler way of relating to breath, body, and mind. He then turns toward refuge, showing how to rest in what is already here rather than trying to outthink uncertainty.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Letting the Dharma Soak into You
item.description:
This guided meditation invites a gentle, receptive relationship with practice rather than an effort to force it. Matthew Brensilver emphasizes settling into the body, meeting agitation with patience and quiet love, and tending tenderly to any sense of distance from the Dharma. The talk later turns to recollecting Buddha, Dharma, and Sangha as a way of re-entering sensitivity, urgency, and trust, before ending in a simple offering of the heart with no strings attached.
Chapters: 02:16 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21803/?t=136 05:18 Body and Earth - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21803/?t=318 06:50 Meeting Unsettledness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21803/?t=410 08:56 When Dharma Feels Distant - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21803/?t=536 14:03 Recalling the Triple Gem - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21803/?t=843 16:23 Courage To Keep Looking - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21803/?t=983 23:57 Offering Without Strings - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21803/?t=1437
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Vitality and Release, In Breath, Out Breath
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on how the in-breath and out-breath can be felt as different but complementary ways of practicing: vitality on the in-breath, release on the out-breath. He encourages a steady, simple attention that learns to welcome both alertness and surrender, and then extends that felt sense into the claim that this moment is not a dress rehearsal but the place where life is actually lived. The talk stays close to breath, posture, and the emotional textures of grasping, fear, brightness, and letting go.
Chapters: 00:14 Arriving and orienting - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21763/?t=14 01:49 Vitality and surrender - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21763/?t=109 03:06 Settling the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21763/?t=186 04:50 The in-breath's vividness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21763/?t=290 07:10 Releasing the out-breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21763/?t=430 12:21 Breath and clarity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21763/?t=741 17:31 Letting go of becoming - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21763/?t=1051 24:10 This is our life - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21763/?t=1450
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Vulnerability & Courage
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation on vulnerability as a source of steadiness rather than weakness. The practice moves from settling the body and refreshing curiosity, into reflections on being undefended, allowing experience to pass through without hardening. He closes by linking courage to a balanced middle way and to a heart that can remain open without losing its center.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Soothing, Space, Love
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with gentle self-care and bodily soothing, then gradually opens into spacious awareness. From there, he turns toward the sense of love as something subtle and already present, not something to manufacture. The practice closes by returning whatever arises — even fear — back into that larger field of love.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Thinking as coping
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on how thinking can function as coping, especially when the system feels unsafe or unsettled. He gently leads listeners toward relaxing the body, softening judgment, and meeting imperfection without needing to sort everything out. The practice gradually reframes thought as an old, innocent survival response and opens into a more welcoming relationship with silence.
Chapters: 00:12 Lineage and trust - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21623/?t=12 00:52 Settling the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21623/?t=52 03:20 Blessing imperfection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21623/?t=200 05:42 Safety and urgency - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21623/?t=342 10:30 Thinking as coping - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21623/?t=630 13:06 Peace with imperfection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21623/?t=786 18:59 Innocent thinking - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21623/?t=1139 19:59 Silence welcomes you - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21623/?t=1199
Source: AudioDharma

item.itunes:author: Matthew Brensilver
item.itunes:summary:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on how thinking can function as coping, especially when the system feels unsafe or unsettled. He gently leads listeners toward relaxing the body, softening judgment, and meeting imperfection without needing to sort everything out. The practice gradually reframes thought as an old, innocent survival response and opens into a more welcoming relationship with silence.
Chapters: 00:12 Lineage and trust - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21623/?t=12 00:52 Settling the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21623/?t=52 03:20 Blessing imperfection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21623/?t=200 05:42 Safety and urgency - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21623/?t=342 10:30 Thinking as coping - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21623/?t=630 13:06 Peace with imperfection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21623/?t=786 18:59 Innocent thinking - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21623/?t=1139 19:59 Silence welcomes you - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21623/?t=1199
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/21623

item.title: Love & Rest Daylong - Tranquility, Shinzen Young's Do-Nothing Practice, Intro and Guided
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores tranquility as a necessary form of protection, not avoidance, and frames the path as a balance between seclusion and opening. He then moves through concrete ways of settling the body and senses—breath, gravity, silence, visual blankness—before introducing Shinzen Young’s do-nothing practice as a rest from controlling attention. The talk gradually turns from skillful stabilization into a guided period of letting experience be, including the release of self-correction and the recognition that there is often nothing to improve in the moment.
Chapters: 00:00 Tranquility and insight - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21699/?t=0 02:25 Protection and sensitivity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21699/?t=145 05:24 Why settling matters - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21699/?t=324 09:52 Breath and body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21699/?t=592 12:31 Listening to silence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21699/?t=751 15:28 Do-nothing practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21699/?t=928 22:58 Guided release - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21699/?t=1378 40:25 No good or bad meditation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21699/?t=2425
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Stabilizing; Compassion
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation that begins with settling the body in a comfortable, alert posture and gently questioning the habit of vigilance. The practice moves into sensing breath throughout the body, especially neglected or tense areas, before opening into compassion for one’s own pain. He emphasizes a patient, steady love that does not try to erase suffering, but meets it with care and presence.

item.title: Guided Meditation: In the Presence of Love (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by settling the body with fuller breaths and a felt sense of love, then repeatedly returns to gravity, breath, and the present moment. Matthew Brensilver gently explores how hidden agendas, self-fixing, and projection can create tension, and he invites a more direct trust in awareness that is not transactional. The talk closes by widening practice into daily life, with an emphasis on tenderness, walking, and letting wisdom and love meet the human condition.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relax into Alertness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on relaxing without losing clarity, beginning with a sense of sangha and settling into the body. He repeatedly reframes ease as a way of becoming more vivid, more attentive, and more genuinely alert, rather than drifting into dullness. The practice moves from balancing tiredness and arousal to resting the heart, noticing the mind’s states, and letting awareness do some of the work.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Gentleness & Equanimity
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on how gentleness and equanimity can work together: honest about what is here, and non-violent toward experience. The practice moves from settling into silence and breath, through softening the impulse to control, toward a deeper trust in allowing pleasant and unpleasant to come and go. Near the end, he points to a quiet contrast between clinging and love, and suggests that not trying to do anything with the mind may be the most gentle move of all.
Chapters: 00:12 Held by Silence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21412/?t=12 01:14 Radical Honesty - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21412/?t=74 03:22 Not Fighting to Heal - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21412/?t=202 07:03 Gentle Strength - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21412/?t=423 10:03 Connecting, Not Controlling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21412/?t=603 14:41 Hands Soft, Clear - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21412/?t=881 18:51 Knowing the Mind - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21412/?t=1131 21:40 Clinging and Love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21412/?t=1300

item.title: Guided Meditation: Tranquility
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation oriented toward settling the body into tranquility without losing aliveness. The practice moves from posture and breath into the belly, then into working gently with hyper-arousal, urgency, and scattered attention. Later he invites resting with whatever pleasantness appears so that ease and tranquility can reinforce one another.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Concentration, Clarity, Equanimity
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation framed by gratitude for practice, then builds the session around three qualities of attention: concentration, clarity, and equanimity. He uses simple images and practical instructions to show how attention can stabilize, sharpen, and become less reactive to pleasure, pain, and change. The meditation gradually turns toward a more open stance: meeting each moment without landing on conclusions, reference points, or grasping.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Effort as Not-Self
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on effort as a kind of not-self practice: instead of pushing harder, the instruction is to stop what can be stopped and receive the breath more openly. The talk explores how meditation can crystallize a sense of a separate doer, and then gently redirects attention toward a deeper, less anxious source of practice. After the instruction, the guidance settles into extended periods of silence punctuated by reminders to be seated and to avoid harsh, moralistic self-talk.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling and Stopping - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21242/?t=0 00:43 Freedom Already Here - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21242/?t=43 02:36 How Effort Becomes Self - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21242/?t=156 05:06 Relaxing the Impulse - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21242/?t=306 09:36 Receiving the Breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21242/?t=576 14:15 Choosing the Right Counsel - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21242/?t=855
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Intimacy with Breathing
item.description:
This guided meditation stays close to the breath while exploring how attention can become more intimate with sensation. Matthew Brensilver invites listeners to notice the breath as fresh, immediate, and sensed through the whole body rather than held as an abstract object. The practice also makes room for tiredness, loose ends, and sticky emotions, returning again and again to breathing as a simple home base.

Matthew Brensilver invites listeners to notice the breath as fresh, immediate, and sensed through the whole body rather than held as an abstract object. The practice also makes room for tiredness, loose ends, and sticky emotions, returning again and again to breathing as a simple home base.
Chapters: 00:00 Arriving With Breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21240/?t=0 00:56 Images and Sensation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21240/?t=56 03:25 Thin Slices of Time - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21240/?t=205 06:10 Breath Awakens the Senses - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21240/?t=370 07:45 Peace and Refuge - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21240/?t=465 13:29 Forgiving the Mind - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21240/?t=809 15:28 Home in the Breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21240/?t=928 19:14 Sticky Thoughts to Breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21240/?t=1154
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Breath, Pleasure, Opening
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with a gentle invitation to soften the face and notice a simple, almost hidden smile. From there, Matthew Brensilver develops a practice of allowing pleasure to be present alongside difficulty, using the breath as a place to meet aliveness without forcing anything to disappear. The talk gradually widens into open awareness, then loosens even the instructions themselves so the practice can rest in sound, sensation, and trust.
Chapters: 00:10 Settling with a Smile - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21233/?t=10 01:40 Joy with Difficulty - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21233/?t=100 03:56 Relaxing and Receiving - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21233/?t=236 08:13 Breath and Pleasure - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21233/?t=493 12:57 An Anchor Everywhere - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21233/?t=777 14:45 Opening to Awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21233/?t=885 20:41 Letting Instructions Go - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21233/?t=1241
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Not Predicting Me
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation built around softening the habit of predicting who we are from one moment to the next. He points to the “song of self” as something that can be noticed rather than believed, and uses that noticing to make breathing and body awareness feel less crowded. The practice gradually turns toward a quieter, more global wakefulness in which experience is allowed to be wider than any fixed story.
Chapters: 00:00 Arrival - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21224/?t=0 01:00 Predicting Ourselves - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21224/?t=60 02:57 Opening to Not-Knowing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21224/?t=177 06:05 The Song of Self - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21224/?t=365 10:46 Love It to Death - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21224/?t=646 12:59 Settling Into Breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21224/?t=779 25:21 Global Wakefulness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21224/?t=1521
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relaxing into the Moment
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on relaxing into the moment with honesty rather than trying to fix experience. The practice moves from settling the breath and body to meeting thoughts and worries with less struggle, then widens into a steadier sense of refuge and trust. By the end, the emphasis is on honoring the moment as a form of healing in itself.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relaxing into Love
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by orienting the body toward ease without losing wakefulness, then moves into breath, heart center, and a softening toward love. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to the idea that when the mind and body settle, awareness can take on the hue of kindness, and he invites a willingness to feel whatever is present—joy, sorrow, pain, or tenderness—without contracting around it. The practice widens into phrases such as “May I be well” and “May all beings be well,” ending in a quiet emphasis on non-violence, refuge, and benevolent wish.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling and posture - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21198/?t=0 01:25 Breath as softening - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21198/?t=85 03:41 Awareness with love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21198/?t=221 05:32 Heart posture - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21198/?t=332 10:09 Bathing pain in care - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21198/?t=609 14:49 Lovingkindness phrases - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21198/?t=889
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Saturday Guided Sit
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided sit centered on feeling-tone: noticing the body, relaxing the impulse to do more, and sensing how arousal and valence shape experience. He repeatedly returns to receiving experience rather than building an action plan from it, and later connects that to the Buddha’s teaching that all dhammas converge on feeling. The closing widens into practical retreat advice about silence, habit, and staying close to practice as the day unfolds.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Thursday Guided Meditation
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by normalizing fear, bracing, and doubt, inviting them to be met with kindness rather than treated as a problem. It then settles into breath as an anchor, widening into the body as a whole, and later turns toward the way attention drifts into memory, anticipation, and the impulse to fix things. The practice closes with a softer sense of surrender and a hint of how intentions can line up when effort becomes less forceful.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Formal Opening Part 1 (sit)
item.description:
A brief opening for retreat, centered on beginning with a 15-minute sit and then moving through logistics for the days ahead. Matthew Brensilver covers seating, hearing assistance, the basic alternation of sitting and walking meditation, and the schedule for instruction, talks, reflections, and discussion groups. The talk ends by naming the value of everyone having set aside time for retreat.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Feeling and Verbal Thinking
item.description:
This guided meditation opens by questioning the impulse to reach for technique and inviting a more undefended, surrendering posture. From there, Matthew Brensilver leads attention into bodily feeling, emotional charge, and the way inner speech can be heard as sound rather than obeyed automatically. The meditation gradually widens into a quiet, patient awareness of the relationship between feelings and words, ending with an emphasis on allowing experience to unfold with blessing and equanimity.
Chapters: 00:00 Arriving and Settling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21104/?t=0 02:40 Letting Go of Technique - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21104/?t=160 05:44 Breath and Release - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21104/?t=344 07:45 Feeling in the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21104/?t=465 11:02 Patience and Permission - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21104/?t=662 14:19 Hearing Thoughts - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21104/?t=859 19:18 Feelings and Stories - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21104/?t=1158 23:56 Equanimity and Blessing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21104/?t=1436
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: You cannot know oneself while fixing oneself
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on resting rather than fixing, beginning with posture, grounding, and the breath as a way of settling the body-mind. The practice gradually opens into a quiet inquiry into samsara, fear, and the urge to make meaning, with repeated reminders that what arises can be met without willfulness. Near the end, the instruction turns toward simple attentiveness: silence as anchor, and even noise met with infinite patience.
Chapters: 00:36 Settling the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21063/?t=36 03:59 Breath and ease - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21063/?t=239 05:58 Putting down the project - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21063/?t=358 09:14 Simple, beckoning practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21063/?t=554 15:41 Phenomena and fear - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21063/?t=941 17:23 Wisp-like experience - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21063/?t=1043 23:28 Silence and patience - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-21063/?t=1408
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Pervasiveness of Craving
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation on craving, control, and the tendency to shape experience. The practice moves from settling the body into a posture of surrender, to noticing the tension of holding on, to softening around heartache and the wish for life to be different. He repeatedly returns to the possibility that awareness can be trusted, and that this moment can be received as a source of wisdom rather than a problem to solve.
Chapters: 00:56 Settling Into Posture - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20987/?t=56 03:21 Noticing Holding On - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20987/?t=201 05:59 Surrendering to This Moment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20987/?t=359 09:03 Breath as Pleasure - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20987/?t=543 13:12 Responsibility Without Power - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20987/?t=792 15:30 Trusting Awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20987/?t=930 20:21 Receiving Wisdom - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20987/?t=1221
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20987
item.itunes:author: Matthew Brensilver
item.itunes:summary:
Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation on craving, control, and the tendency to shape experience. The practice moves from settling the body into a posture of surrender, to noticing the tension of holding on, to softening around heartache and the wish for life to be different. He repeatedly returns to the possibility that awareness can be trusted, and that this moment can be received as a source of wisdom rather than a problem to solve.
Chapters: 00:56 Settling Into Posture - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20987/?t=56 03:21 Noticing Holding On - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20987/?t=201 05:59 Surrendering to This Moment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20987/?t=359 09:03 Breath as Pleasure - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20987/?t=543 13:12 Responsibility Without Power - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20987/?t=792 15:30 Trusting Awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20987/?t=930 20:21 Receiving Wisdom - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20987/?t=1221
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20987

item.title: Guided Meditation: Too Busy Knowing
item.description:
A guided meditation built around relaxing vigilance and trusting the body’s capacity to meet experience. Matthew Brensilver invites a quieter mode of attention: less patrol, more surrender, while keeping the mind clear and awake. The practice returns several times to impermanence and to the felt sense of experience changing moment by moment, without freezing it into something solid.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling and Relaxing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20921/?t=12 04:10 Safety and Relinquishment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20921/?t=250 06:56 Anchors and Clarity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20921/?t=416 09:23 Snagged by Experience - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20921/?t=563 10:07 Moment by Moment Knowing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20921/?t=607 20:58 Too Busy Knowing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20921/?t=1258
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20921

item.title: Guided Meditation: Sitting in the Bones
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on settling attention into the body, especially the felt sense of the bones, heaviness, and gravity. The practice moves from fuller breathing and relaxation into a steadier, mountain-like groundedness, then broadens into the freedom to let experience arise and pass without strain. In the closing, he brings in loving-kindness as a quiet ethical and emotional dimension of the practice.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling the Breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20864/?t=12 02:30 Sitting in the Bones - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20864/?t=150 04:11 Gravity and Earth - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20864/?t=251 06:01 Attention Downward - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20864/?t=361 09:34 Mountain Stability - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20864/?t=574 11:01 Stop Doing So Much - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20864/?t=661 17:28 The Self Can Relax - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20864/?t=1048 23:11 From Rest to Metta - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20864/?t=1391
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Landing in the Breath and Soundscape
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation and retreat orientation that begins with fear, softness, and the need to feel safe enough to simplify attention. He then moves through breath practice, soundscape awareness, and walking meditation, returning again and again to the idea that practice is less about willpower than willingness. Near the end, he frames boredom, overwhelm, and even ordinary retreat difficulty as part of the field to be known with gentleness.
Chapters: 00:01 Settling fear - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-85473/?t=1 04:29 Mood in awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-85473/?t=269 10:03 Breath as refuge - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-85473/?t=603 17:02 Thinking without struggle - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-85473/?t=1022 24:06 Awake and relaxed - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-85473/?t=1446 29:59 Listening to soundscape - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-85473/?t=1799 37:50 Retreat rhythm - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-85473/?t=2270 45:25 Whole-day awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-85473/?t=2725 52:57 Closing encouragement - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-85473/?t=3177
Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Guided Meditation: Non-tension
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on non-tension: noticing how the body and mind respond to conditions, and learning not to wait for things to be perfect before letting go. The practice moves from settling into bodily awareness, to relaxing clinging and bracing, to resting with impermanence without pushing or pulling against it. The closing section uses a repeated image of falling into the present, with less baggage and fewer places for attention to snag.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening and welcome - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20758/?t=0 01:45 Temperature and the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20758/?t=105 04:13 Not waiting for perfect - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20758/?t=253 12:05 Awareness in the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20758/?t=725 14:54 Relaxing clinging - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20758/?t=894 18:52 Impermanence as practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20758/?t=1132 23:28 Falling into presence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20758/?t=1408
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relaxing and Falling Awake
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on settling the body and mind without forcing ease. The practice moves from posture and releasing the day, through soft attention to breath and sound, toward a gentler understanding of relaxation as relinquishment rather than control. He returns repeatedly to the idea that tension is bound up with grasping, and that waking up may happen in a way closer to falling asleep than to trying harder.
Chapters: 00:29 Settling Into Posture - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20693/?t=29 01:51 Laying Down the Day - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20693/?t=111 04:19 Blessing and Release - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20693/?t=259 06:17 Breath and Sound - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20693/?t=377 11:05 Tension and Holding - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20693/?t=665 15:37 Relinquishing Control - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20693/?t=937 21:07 Relaxing as Exhale - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20693/?t=1267 24:30 Falling Awake - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20693/?t=1470
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Relaxation and the Tension of Dukkha, Self, Clinging
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that uses relaxation as a way to notice the subtle tension bound up with clinging, selfing, greed, hatred, and delusion. He moves from an image of a sleepy child asking for comfort to a quieter investigation of how deep release can reveal what the body-mind has been holding. The practice is gentle but pointed: soften, breathe, let gravity help, and keep putting things down until awareness feels less burdened.
Chapters: 00:14 Opening story - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20627/?t=14 01:43 Relaxation and suffering - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20627/?t=103 05:36 Right here to sit - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20627/?t=336 07:23 Let tension speak - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20627/?t=443 10:21 Gravity and ease - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20627/?t=621 13:35 Self as contraction - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20627/?t=815 18:28 Clinging and hate - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20627/?t=1108 21:42 Delusion and mortality - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20627/?t=1302 24:15 Putting things down - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20627/?t=1455
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Self and Willfulness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation that keeps returning to a simple question: what happens if we stop trying to make meditation happen? He gently examines how willfulness can create a self that manages experience, and invites a softer trust in awareness, breath, body, and sound. The practice gradually shifts from effort and suspicion toward surrender, letting doingness dissolve into openness.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Tranquility
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on tranquility as both settling and clearer seeing. He begins with breath, bodily ease, and releasing clinging, then widens the practice to listening to silence, resting in the visual field, and allowing attention to soften around experience. A brief reference to Mother Teresa’s description of prayer helps frame the practice as a quiet, receptive listening, without requiring any religious belief.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Goodnight Moon
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation built around the rhythm of Goodnight Moon, using the book’s repeated bedtime refrain as a gentle model for letting experience rest. He invites listeners to say goodnight to memories, pain, thoughts, and feelings, then to settle into breathing and open awareness without forcing anything away. The practice gradually widens from soft release into a quiet sense of presence, effortlessness, and brightness.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening the Meditation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20468/?t=0 02:36 Saying Goodnight - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20468/?t=156 09:15 Settling With Breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20468/?t=555 11:49 Open Awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20468/?t=709 15:04 Not Rejection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20468/?t=904 16:08 Resting in Presence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20468/?t=968 26:02 Fatigue and Brightness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20468/?t=1562
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/20468

item.title: Guided Meditation: Feeling
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation centered on feeling, beginning with breath and a gentle turn away from compulsive problem-solving. The practice moves through the affective roots of reactivity, the ways feeling gets turned into story, and the invitation to let experience unwind without feeding it with thought. Later he broadens into retreat-specific reflection on futurelessness, death, effort, boredom, and how to keep practice steady without turning effort into self-strain.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling With Breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-83891/?t=0 02:14 Problems and Dissolving - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-83891/?t=134 03:10 Commitment To This Moment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-83891/?t=190 06:25 Wanting And Knowing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-83891/?t=385 09:46 Feeling And Reactivity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-83891/?t=586 13:46 Stories From Feeling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-83891/?t=826 20:12 Non-Thinking And Release - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-83891/?t=1212 23:11 Clinging And Heaven - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-83891/?t=1391 28:35 Futurelessness And Death - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-83891/?t=1715

item.title: Guided Meditation: Convergence of Insight and Love (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver frames the talk around the convergence of insight and love: mindfulness as a careful deconstruction of experience, and the brahma-viharas as the heart’s place to rest. He moves through the four “species of love” — loving-kindness, compassion, sympathetic joy, and equanimity — showing how each meets a different kind of life moment, from goodness to suffering to helplessness. The guided portion then turns inward, inviting listeners to stop measuring, sense what is needed, and offer phrases of goodwill that soften the heart rather than harden it.
Chapters: 00:00 Insight and love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-83872/?t=0 05:13 The four abodes - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-83872/?t=313 08:29 Compassion and equanimity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-83872/?t=509 12:40 Letting love be home - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-83872/?t=760 14:31 Dropping specialness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-83872/?t=871 22:44 Offering goodwill - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-83872/?t=1364 27:55 Softening into the heart - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-83872/?t=1675
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/83872/

item.title: Guided Meditation: The Breath and Awareness don't depend on you
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation that begins with settling the body and reframing the breath as something that does not depend on the usual sense of “me.” From there he turns toward awareness itself, emphasizing that it also is not owned or controlled, and uses the language of emptiness to soften selfing. The back half of the talk lingers in long repetitions and then opens into an image of egoic “proprioception,” showing how a sense of self is continually maintained until awareness becomes less identified and more spacious.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20353/?t=0 01:15 Breath Beyond Self - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20353/?t=75 03:27 Awareness Receives - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20353/?t=207 05:22 Emptiness Repeated - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20353/?t=322 11:07 Ego's Bureaucracy - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20353/?t=667 13:08 Same Stuff, Rising - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20353/?t=788 17:42 Egoic Proprioception - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20353/?t=1062 20:22 Less and Less Me - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20353/?t=1222
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Awareness and Effortlessness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores the shift from narrating experience to resting in awareness itself, drawing on Thomas Metzinger to describe silence as non-conceptual, non-reactive knowing. The talk moves from theory into guided sitting, where effort, choicelessness, and relaxation are treated as supports that can eventually fall away. Later he turns toward equanimity, frictionlessness, and the sense that the moment is no longer a problem to solve but something to trust.
Chapters: 00:30 Narrative Self - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20281/?t=30 02:23 Silent Knowing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20281/?t=143 05:07 Instructions And Fruit - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20281/?t=307 07:18 Settling To Sit - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20281/?t=438 09:17 Friction And Equanimity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20281/?t=557 12:32 Effort Toward Release - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20281/?t=752 18:19 Trusting Silence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20281/?t=1099 25:26 Resting Safely - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20281/?t=1526
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Language as Safety
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation framed around a gentle critique of our reliance on language for comfort and control. The practice moves from settling the body and allowing imperfection, into resting with feeling and breath without immediately turning experience into a story. Near the end, he names the felt contrast directly: words can seem like safety, but deeper safety comes from wisdom, love, and learning to stay with uncertainty.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Attention, Effort, Choice and Selfing
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation framed around a subtle paradox: meditation asks for attention, effort, and choice, while also pointing toward choicelessness, effortlessness, and surrender. He keeps returning to the way practice can strengthen a sense of doer and meditator, then gently loosens that grip by treating selfing as something to study rather than defeat. The guidance moves from settling posture and narrowing attention to breathing, through reminders and rebalancing, and toward a quieter release of the need to manage the meditation at all.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Aperture, Vividness, Stability, Effort
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation built around four practical dimensions of practice: intention, attention, vividness, stability, and effort. He repeatedly returns to the sense that meditation is not a workspace for solving problems, but a period of putting down grasping and letting the moment be enough. The sit then moves from a more concrete anchor to a broader, more open awareness, with guidance on how to adjust effort without strain.

item.title: Guided Meditation: What's Already Here?
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver opens with a brief reflection on war, cooperation, and the idea that peace may be something human beings have to invent. He then turns the talk into a guided meditation centered on a recurring instruction: stop looking, and let awareness, breath, silence, and even healing "find" you. The practice keeps returning to the ordinary fact that what we seek is already present, and that freedom and suffering meet in the same place.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Faith in Silence
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on learning to trust silence instead of using thought as a constant coping strategy. He gently argues that the body’s signals, the mind’s reactions, and the urge to solve everything do not need to be read as threats, and he repeatedly returns to the reassurance that it is okay to let things rest. The practice moves from settling the posture and soothing the body into longer stretches of silence, with reminders to soften, stop over-reading experience, and listen for what tranquility makes possible.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20005/?t=12 02:41 Soothing the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20005/?t=161 05:06 Putting Down Complications - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20005/?t=306 10:18 Faith in Silence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20005/?t=618 15:52 Over-Reading Experience - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20005/?t=952 18:48 Heart Training - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20005/?t=1128 20:05 Listening To Silence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20005/?t=1205 25:20 Extended Quiet - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-20005/?t=1520

item.title: Guided Meditation: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided meditation that keeps returning to receptive listening: allowing experience to be received before it hardens into resistance or identification. The talk moves from settling the body, to noticing the quiet agenda of “me” in meditation, to softening around wanting, control, and the effort to make something happen. Near the end, he frames practice in terms of faith — the possibility that something can hold us even when control is released.

item.itunes:author: Matthew Brensilver
item.itunes:summary:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided meditation that keeps returning to receptive listening: allowing experience to be received before it hardens into resistance or identification. The talk moves from settling the body, to noticing the quiet agenda of “me” in meditation, to softening around wanting, control, and the effort to make something happen. Near the end, he frames practice in terms of faith — the possibility that something can hold us even when control is released.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Attention as Prayer
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver frames this guided meditation around Simone Weil’s line that attention at its highest degree is prayer, then unfolds that idea through body, breath, and sound. The practice moves from a somewhat deliberate, instructed beginning toward a softer sense that awareness is not owned or controlled by a separate meditator. Along the way he weaves in forgiveness, devotion, and the gradual loosening of selfing until attention feels more like surrender than effort.
Chapters: 01:05 Attention as Prayer - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19984/?t=65 02:53 View and Devotion - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19984/?t=173 04:53 Settling the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19984/?t=293 07:38 Forgiveness and Recommitment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19984/?t=458 10:44 Love in Disguise - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19984/?t=644 14:11 Less Control, More Grace - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19984/?t=851 17:56 Letting Awareness Find Us - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19984/?t=1076 26:12 Groundless and Held - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19984/?t=1572
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19984

item.title: Guided Meditation: Attention and Sustaining Openness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on two linked skills: training attention and sustaining openness when intensity arises. The talk repeatedly returns to how awareness narrows into problem-solving and how practice can gently re-open the field, with attention to the body, breath, soundscape, and a sense of okayness. Rather than building a conceptual map, it keeps pointing back to direct experience, patience, and relinquishment as ways to meet ordinary and subtle stress.
Chapters: 00:00 Practice frame - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19970/?t=0 02:50 Settle into posture - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19970/?t=170 05:13 Intensity and collapse - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19970/?t=313 09:38 Reverence for dukkha - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19970/?t=578 13:43 Good enough awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19970/?t=823 19:47 Re-establish openness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19970/?t=1187 26:31 Not solving - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19970/?t=1591
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Establishing Tranquility
item.description:
This guided meditation settles into posture, then turns attention toward silence as a quieter guide than the mind's usual instructions. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly narrows the attentional field to the breath, inviting the listener to relax what can be relaxed, allow what remains, and feel how even a small measure of tranquility changes the way experience appears. Near the end, he connects that shift to a Buddhist teaching that mind conditions what is known, closing with a gentle emphasis on refuge, nourishment, and a softer inner gaze.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling Into Silence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19960/?t=12 02:01 Relaxing the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19960/?t=121 03:00 Tranquility Changes View - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19960/?t=180 06:22 Narrowing Attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19960/?t=382 11:22 Faith in Silence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19960/?t=682 20:52 Stabilizing the Inner Gaze - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19960/?t=1252 26:04 Tranquility as Refuge - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19960/?t=1564 28:22 Closing Bells - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19960/?t=1702
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation - Deep Letting Go
item.description:
This guided meditation explores a deep kind of letting go by loosening the sense of being stuck in time, self-story, and reactive wanting. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly invites the listener to rest outside memory and possibility, and to notice how clinging helps create the very sense of time it seems to live within. The practice moves gradually from a question of heart posture into spaciousness, emptiness, and a quieter encounter with experience.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation - Mindfulness of Feeling
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on mindfulness of feeling, beginning with rain, body awareness, and the simple instruction to let go. He then turns toward the Buddha’s teaching that all dharma converge on feeling, using it to distinguish raw feeling from the stories, urgencies, and self-judgments that can grow around it. The practice moves gently from soothing and breathing with sensation toward equanimity, listening, and a more loving relationship to whatever arises.

item.title: Guided Meditation - Landing in the Breath
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that moves from settling fear and bracing into a close, steady contact with breathing. He emphasizes that discomfort, thought, and uncertainty can be met as present experience rather than warnings about the future, and gradually opens into walking meditation and the continuity of mindfulness across posture changes. The talk closes by normalizing the ordinary difficulties of retreat and framing surrender, simplicity, and patience as part of the practice itself.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Resting In Care
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on resting in care, beginning by inviting a felt sense of love, gratitude, compassion, or even grief as a doorway into the heart. From there he explores how care can color the whole body and make each breath feel significant, then turns toward the freedom that comes when love is steady enough to hold what is not love without forcing anything. The practice closes with a gentle wish for release from suffering.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Resting In What We Cannot Stop
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on resting in awareness as something that is already present and not fully under personal control. The talk moves from settling the body and appreciating being alive, into a careful distinction between the effort of practice and the effortlessness of awareness itself. He repeatedly points back to the phrase that whatever cannot be stopped is where to rest, treating mindfulness as a kind of grace rather than a project of will.
Chapters: 00:52 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19425/?t=52 04:00 Gratitude for Awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19425/?t=240 09:32 Effort and Ease - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19425/?t=572 11:37 Awareness Beyond Will - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19425/?t=697 20:23 Mindfulness as Grace - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19425/?t=1223 26:15 Resting in What Cannot Stop - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19425/?t=1575
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19425

item.title: Guided Meditation: Letting the Moment Soften You
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on the felt sense of softening rather than forcing concentration. He moves through breath, body, thought, emotion, and sound as different ways the moment can be met, with repeated encouragement to meet experience with blessing, awareness, and release. The practice ends by framing this softening as rehearsal for freedom: not pretending discomfort is pleasant, but learning to let go again and again.
Chapters: 00:12 Arriving - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19422/?t=12 01:13 Entering the moment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19422/?t=73 02:20 Finding the breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19422/?t=140 03:39 Meeting the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19422/?t=219 09:06 Softened by phenomena - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19422/?t=546 11:17 When suppleness is lost - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19422/?t=677 18:18 Letting go in practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19422/?t=1098 26:02 Rehearsing freedom - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19422/?t=1562
Source: AudioDharma

item.itunes:summary:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on the felt sense of softening rather than forcing concentration. He moves through breath, body, thought, emotion, and sound as different ways the moment can be met, with repeated encouragement to meet experience with blessing, awareness, and release. The practice ends by framing this softening as rehearsal for freedom: not pretending discomfort is pleasant, but learning to let go again and again.
Chapters: 00:12 Arriving - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19422/?t=12 01:13 Entering the moment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19422/?t=73 02:20 Finding the breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19422/?t=140 03:39 Meeting the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19422/?t=219 09:06 Softened by phenomena - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19422/?t=546 11:17 When suppleness is lost - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19422/?t=677 18:18 Letting go in practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19422/?t=1098 26:02 Rehearsing freedom - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19422/?t=1562
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/19422

item.title: Guided Meditation: Renouncing the Narrative Impulse
item.description:
This guided meditation invites steady attention into bodily feeling and the tendency to turn sensation into a story. Matthew Brensilver emphasizes renouncing the narrative impulse—not by suppressing thought, but by meeting feeling as feeling and softening the reflex to search for meaning, threat, or command. The practice gradually opens toward defenselessness, spaciousness, and the possibility that nothing needs to happen right now.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening and Welcome - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19406/?t=0 00:50 Inviting Goodwill - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19406/?t=50 03:13 Landing in the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19406/?t=193 06:56 Feeling Without Story - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19406/?t=416 10:23 Narrative and Vigilance - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19406/?t=623 16:18 Experimenting with Defenselessness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19406/?t=978 20:50 Nothing Needs to Happen - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19406/?t=1250 27:46 Distilling Wisdom - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19406/?t=1666
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Not updating your autobiography
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation and reflection on not turning each moment into a continuation of the personal story. He repeatedly returns to the body, breath, and sensory precision, then widens into equanimity, non-clinging, and a kind of futurelessness that lets this moment be enough. The talk moves gently between instruction and reflection, emphasizing surrender without erasing memory or life history.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19397/?t=12 02:37 Body and Breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19397/?t=157 06:19 Awareness Spreads - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19397/?t=379 10:20 Not the Next Chapter - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19397/?t=620 12:09 Letting Go of Story - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19397/?t=729 19:07 Equanimity and Non-clinging - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19397/?t=1147 21:17 Self as Hologram - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19397/?t=1277 26:58 Futurelessness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19397/?t=1618

item.title: Guided Meditation - The Warmth of Love
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on warmth as a lived quality rather than a metaphor, moving gently from settling the body into the wider field of love, compassion, joy, and equanimity. The guidance emphasizes allowing, forgiving, and making a truce with imperfection, then opens into phrases of well-wishing and a quiet acceptance of what cannot be controlled. A brief closing chant may be present near the end, though its exact framing is not fully clear.

item.title: Guided Meditation - Love and Grief
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by settling the body and then opens into a reflection on Leonard Cohen’s “Treaty,” using the song’s longing for peace as a way into the meeting of love and grief. Matthew then explores grief as something that may belong within practice, even suggesting it could be understood as a kind of fifth Brahma-vihara, before turning to impermanence and how grief can mature into compassion, gratitude, and reverence. The session closes with metta phrases and a final emphasis on how intimate contact with our own suffering can deepen our response to others.

item.title: Friday Morning Instructions
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores how perception is shaped by motivation, then turns toward letting go as a way of seeing more clearly. He moves from the innocence of clinging and the body’s affective patterns into a steadier, more compassionate relationship with experience, including the repeated refrain that we are not doing our life wrong. The talk closes on trust: awareness is not a story, and when we fall, love may be what catches us.
Chapters: 00:01 Wanting Shapes Seeing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19240/?t=1 01:37 Letting Go - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19240/?t=97 03:14 Clinging and Self-Knowledge - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19240/?t=194 09:21 Affect and Innocence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19240/?t=561 17:52 Suffering and Delusion - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19240/?t=1072 20:42 Mind In Images - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19240/?t=1242 27:43 Awareness Is Not Story - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19240/?t=1663 29:50 Faith and Being Held - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-19240/?t=1790
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Wednesday morning instructions
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet set of instructions for sitting with experience as something moving and changing rather than fixed. He keeps returning to posture, nervous system, and the practice of meeting feeling with awareness, patience, and sensitivity, then opens that ground into equanimity, love, and a frank view of the human condition. The talk includes a few clearly garbled transcript passages; the teaching itself remains steady and coherent.

item.title: Morning Instructions: Landing in the body breathing
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver opens this first guided sit of a week-long retreat by inviting listeners to set down the background project of self-management and settle into the body. The practice unfolds as a steady return to softening—face, shoulders, belly, and breath—along with a gentler relationship to attention, fatigue, and restlessness. In the second half, he widens the frame to walking practice, continuity through the day, and the patience to keep practicing when nothing seems to be happening.

item.title: Reflections and Guided Meditation on Mudita
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver reflects on mudita, or appreciative joy, by first grounding it in tenderness, grief, and the ways the heart can stay open without pretending. He then turns to comparison and envy as forms of suffering that support a false self, and shows how practice can loosen that grip through compassion, softness, and rejoicing in another’s welfare. The talk closes with a guided meditation that begins with an easy person, then moves toward someone who stirs comparison, and ends in self-care and blessing.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening reflection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80555/?t=0 04:00 Tears and tenderness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80555/?t=240 06:19 Joy without pretending - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80555/?t=379 08:54 What mudita is - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80555/?t=534 12:15 Selfing through comparison - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80555/?t=735 15:40 Sharing another’s good - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80555/?t=940 20:31 Guided practice begins - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80555/?t=1231 24:53 Working with envy - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80555/?t=1493 29:07 Softening and closing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80555/?t=1747
Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Guided meditation: all phenomena as false alarms
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that keeps returning to a simple instruction: stop the extra doing, and meet experience with care. He frames sounds, thoughts, body sensations, and emotional surges as “false alarms,” inviting listeners not to turn every feeling into a story about the self. The talk gradually widens into a quiet teaching on confidence, emptiness, and love: if phenomena are not owned, they can soften the heart rather than harm it.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the Doing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80546/?t=0 03:19 Care for the Heart - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80546/?t=199 05:54 False Alarms - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80546/?t=354 10:12 Confidence and Softening - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80546/?t=612 14:54 Emotion and Urgency - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80546/?t=894 21:06 No Story Needed - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80546/?t=1266 32:28 Love After Selfing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80546/?t=1948 40:51 Questions and Closing Guidance - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80546/?t=2451
Source: Dharma Seed

He frames sounds, thoughts, body sensations, and emotional surges as “false alarms,” inviting listeners not to turn every feeling into a story about the self. The talk gradually widens into a quiet teaching on confidence, emptiness, and love: if phenomena are not owned, they can soften the heart rather than harm it.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the Doing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80546/?t=0 03:19 Care for the Heart - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80546/?t=199 05:54 False Alarms - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80546/?t=354 10:12 Confidence and Softening - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80546/?t=612 14:54 Emotion and Urgency - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80546/?t=894 21:06 No Story Needed - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80546/?t=1266 32:28 Love After Selfing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80546/?t=1948 40:51 Questions and Closing Guidance - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80546/?t=2451

item.title: Metta Practice
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver presents metta as something more than a pleasant mood: a capacity that makes insight practice bearable, especially when the heart meets the full intensity of suffering. He moves through the four brahmaviharas as four distinct kinds of love, then explains how metta can cultivate warmth, steady the mind, or bring buried grief and fear to the surface for healing. The talk closes with a simple practice of repeating phrases, listening for their effect, and widening the field of care to others without denying war, oppression, or loss.
Chapters: 00:00 Love and Insight - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80506/?t=0 02:28 Four Species of Love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80506/?t=148 06:33 Why Love Matters - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80506/?t=393 10:56 Meeting What Hurts - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80506/?t=656 12:45 Settling the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80506/?t=765 16:55 Remembering Goodness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80506/?t=1015 25:32 Abiding in Kindness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80506/?t=1532 31:33 Extending the Wish - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80506/?t=1893 37:04 A Sublime Abiding - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-80506/?t=2224

item.title: Guided Meditation: Deconstructing Sensory Experience
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation built around a simple three-part discernment: seeing, hearing, and feeling. After introducing the method and naming the tendency to turn practice into self-improvement, he leads a gradual scanning of visual thought, inner speech, and affect in the body, with repeated emphasis on noticing arising and passing without collapsing into identification. The practice closes by cycling through the three sensory spheres and resting in the untangling of “I am-ness.”
Chapters: 00:12 Opening the method - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18180/?t=12 01:43 Seeing, hearing, feeling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18180/?t=103 05:50 Settling and surrendering - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18180/?t=350 08:28 Noticing visual thought - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18180/?t=508 11:38 Listening to inner speech - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18180/?t=698 17:25 Feeling in the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18180/?t=1045 19:52 Opening to all three - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18180/?t=1192 24:16 Cycling and untangling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18180/?t=1456
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Awareness Without Willfulness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that keeps returning to the difference between effort and willfulness. After a brief, candid opening about being emotionally caught up in a basketball game, he settles into posture, body, and breath, then invites a steady awareness of the residues of the day without trying to fix them. The practice gradually shifts toward relaxing the impulse to do something to experience, resting instead in an awareness that feels less personal and less effortful.
Chapters: 00:35 Opening candor - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18167/?t=35 02:29 Settle the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18167/?t=149 04:40 Taking stock - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18167/?t=280 07:03 Allowing residue - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18167/?t=423 07:59 Breath and balance - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18167/?t=479 11:41 Effort and grace - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18167/?t=701 17:19 Relaxing the doer - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18167/?t=1039 22:51 Awareness without self - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18167/?t=1371 30:03 Closing bells - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18167/?t=1803

item.title: Guided Meditation: Listening to the Mind of Buddha
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that gradually shifts from settling into the room toward a practice of listening: first to breath, body, sound, and the darkness behind closed eyes, then to a deeper receptivity he links to Leonard Cohen’s hummingbird lines. The talk emphasizes trusting awareness, letting silence beckon, and resting without over-managing experience. It closes by naming practice as a way to stop endlessly coping and simply be awake.
Chapters: 00:11 Arriving and Settling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18176/?t=11 01:30 Silence Beckons - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18176/?t=90 02:58 Simple Objects of Attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18176/?t=178 05:04 Listening to Cohen - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18176/?t=304 06:23 Trusting Awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18176/?t=383 15:23 Forgiveness and Rest - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18176/?t=923 17:33 Patience and Urgency - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18176/?t=1053 23:35 Beyond Coping - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18176/?t=1415

item.title: Guided Meditation: Not Locating Oneself
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation organized around not re-establishing the usual sense of self. He invites a gentle stance of not knowing, then balances attention to the meditation object with attention to how attention is being held. The talk gradually settles into a quiet instruction to let self-references dissolve into the openness of the present moment.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening and welcome - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18177/?t=0 00:30 Settling without knowing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18177/?t=30 01:20 Not locating a self - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18177/?t=80 03:30 Opening practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18177/?t=210 08:09 What and how - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18177/?t=489 09:36 Sustained guidance - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18177/?t=576 18:29 Care and openness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18177/?t=1109 22:10 Closing silence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18177/?t=1330

item.title: Guided Meditation: Entering the Dharma Field
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a gentle guided meditation that begins by settling into posture and letting the breath remind us of the Dharma. The practice moves from opening to the intensity of life, to finding refuge in breathing, and finally to entering a wider "Dharma field" where even mistakes can become part of the path. Near the end, he draws on Ajahn Sucitto’s language to reframe the meditation as stepping into a realm that continually recalls Buddha, Dharma, and Sangha.
Chapters: 00:42 Arriving and settling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18164/?t=42 04:52 Breath as reminder - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18164/?t=292 07:26 Opening and refuge - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18164/?t=446 09:20 Breath as shelter - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18164/?t=560 14:21 Folding thoughts back - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18164/?t=861 18:51 Hope and fear - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18164/?t=1131 21:51 Invoking the Buddha - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18164/?t=1311 25:06 Entering Dharma field - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-18164/?t=1506
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Trajectory of your life and trajectory of freedom
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver frames the meditation around two trajectories: the anxious, habit-driven story of a life, and the quieter training that leads toward freedom. The talk moves from this reflection into guided practice, emphasizing relaxation, sensing the whole body, and meeting feeling states without treating them as evidence that something is wrong. The practice repeatedly returns to softness, awareness, and the possibility that you do not have to become anyone in order to be present.
Chapters: 00:00 Two Trajectories - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-77006/?t=0 06:09 Letting Go - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-77006/?t=369 13:25 Settling Into Practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-77006/?t=805 18:11 Undefended Presence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-77006/?t=1091 23:50 False Alarms - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-77006/?t=1430 29:36 Softening the Heart - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-77006/?t=1776
Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Moved by goodness: Reflections and Guided Metta Meditation (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver begins by contrasting mindfulness as deconstruction with metta as a rebuilding of the heart out of love. He then sketches loving-kindness as a boundless, noncontrolling form of love that is deepened by grief, and he places it within the four divine abodes. The talk moves into a guided metta meditation, first toward self-acceptance and then toward a quieter, gentler blessing practice that treats words as a bell for the body to hear.
Chapters: 00:00 Deconstruction and love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-77000/?t=0 02:17 What love means here - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-77000/?t=137 05:28 The four divine abodes - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-77000/?t=328 09:43 Moved by goodness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-77000/?t=583 12:35 Cultivation and purification - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-77000/?t=755 17:12 Settling into practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-77000/?t=1032 25:15 Self-blessing phrases - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-77000/?t=1515 31:40 Expansiveness of love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-77000/?t=1900
Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Guided Meditation: Not reading the tea leaves of the body
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on meeting body experience without turning it into a puzzle to solve. He repeatedly returns to the idea that sensations, emotions, and alarms can be received as data, then gently released, rather than interpreted as signs of what will happen next. Near the end, he brings in the Mula Sutta to frame each experience as a doorway to Dharma, with liberation rather than self-analysis as the orientation.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening into welcome - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=0 02:43 Receiving the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=163 05:09 Not solving problems - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=309 07:16 Past and future pressure - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=436 14:38 False alarms - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=878 17:01 This moment only - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=1021 23:31 Letting go repeatedly - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=1411 28:53 Liberation in the sutta - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=1733 33:44 Renouncing words - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=2024
Source: AudioDharma

He repeatedly returns to the idea that sensations, emotions, and alarms can be received as data, then gently released, rather than interpreted as signs of what will happen next. Near the end, he brings in the Mula Sutta to frame each experience as a doorway to Dharma, with liberation rather than self-analysis as the orientation.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening into welcome - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=0 02:43 Receiving the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=163 05:09 Not solving problems - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=309 07:16 Past and future pressure - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=436 14:38 False alarms - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=878 17:01 This moment only - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=1021 23:31 Letting go repeatedly - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=1411 28:53 Liberation in the sutta - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=1733 33:44 Renouncing words - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17989/?t=2024
