item.title: Guided Meditation: Settling and Opening
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with a very simple orientation to posture, embodied awareness, and relaxation, then gradually widens into advice for retreat practice. He repeatedly returns to the ways fear, hope, fatigue, and agitation shape the mind, and encourages listeners not to over-interpret what arises on the first day. The talk ends by extending mindfulness into walking practice and daily continuity, with a gentle reminder that one difficult day is not a basis for conclusions.
Chapters: 00:00 Simple settling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17986/?t=0 02:00 Fear and interpretation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17986/?t=120 07:25 Breath as refuge - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17986/?t=445 18:06 Questions dissolve - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17986/?t=1086 23:35 Cycles of breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17986/?t=1415 33:37 Retreat opens the heart - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17986/?t=2017 38:20 Meeting what arises - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17986/?t=2300 41:20 Walking with continuity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17986/?t=2480 51:53 No conclusions today - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17986/?t=3113
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Stability and Change
item.description:
This guided meditation settles into the body, the breath, and the felt support of the earth before opening into a reflection on stability and change. Matthew Brensilver gradually shifts from simple instructions to a quieter contemplation of impermanence, asking what it means to rest in awareness without trying to hold experience still. The talk ends by widening into a river image: life as continuous flow, with brief moments of solidifying and release.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Sensitivity all the way down
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on receptivity, using chitta as a way of understanding the heart-mind as something inherently open and affected. The practice moves from settling the body, to recognizing how we brace against pain and self-protection, and then toward a steadier openness that does not need to land in conclusions. Near the end, he emphasizes that goodness is already here, and that the Dharma can be trusted through direct feeling rather than striving.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17755/?t=0 01:22 Chitta as Receptivity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17755/?t=82 03:22 Porous and Poised - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17755/?t=202 05:28 Always Being Touched - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17755/?t=328 10:00 False Alarms - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17755/?t=600 16:40 Bracing and Protection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17755/?t=1000 18:57 Openness Without Defense - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17755/?t=1137 24:50 Goodness Already Here - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17755/?t=1490
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Openness of Awareness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on how attention shapes experience. He moves from settling the body and softening vigilance into breath-based practices that brighten, soothe, and release, then opens into a wider sense of awareness. The talk closes by contrasting that grounded simplicity with the pull of hope and fear, and how the heart can see things differently when rest and seclusion are present.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Thought as the exhaust from engine of affect
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation built around attunement to present-moment experience, especially the way feeling gives rise to interpretation and mental proliferation. He invites listeners to notice the body’s surges without over-reading them, then repeatedly returns to a single instruction: soften. The practice gradually widens into resting with unpleasantness, letting the Dharma saturate experience, and allowing felt urgency to be understood more gently.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Entering the Dharma
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation centered on “entering the Dharma” as a bodily, felt act rather than a project of self-improvement. The practice moves through settling the body, recollecting Buddha, Dharma, and Sangha, and opening to a softer stance of receptivity and surrender. Thich Nhat Hanh’s image of the nectar settling in a glass becomes a recurring touchstone for letting experience clarify on its own.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17743/?t=0 01:40 Entering the Dharma - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17743/?t=100 04:06 Nectar Settles - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17743/?t=246 07:13 Recollecting Refuge - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17743/?t=433 09:38 Breath and Blessing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17743/?t=578 11:04 Receptive Attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17743/?t=664 16:30 Surrender and Acceptance - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17743/?t=990 23:36 Trusting Openness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17743/?t=1416

item.title: Guided Meditation: Surrendering to the Present
item.description:
In this guided meditation, Matthew Brensilver invites listeners to settle the body, relax the mind, and let awareness gently include whatever is currently unresolved. The practice turns toward the difficulty of clinging and control, suggesting that presence involves making enough peace with experience that attention is no longer pulled away by it. From there, the talk opens into a quieter letting go: hopes and fears remain, but they become less entangling, and the heart can rest.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Letting the Winds of Feeling Blow
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation centered on feeling as something to trust rather than manage or explain. He begins by inviting a careful “Dharma posture,” then gradually turns toward the body’s surges of emotion and impulse, framing practice as learning to let feeling move through without immediately turning it into story. Near the end, he links that surrender to confidence in Buddha, Dhamma, and Sangha, so the practice lands in faith rather than effortful control.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Beginning with Goodness
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with the suggestion that practice can start from a felt sense of goodness already present in experience. Matthew Brensilver then repeatedly returns to the breath as a place of receiving, releasing, and settling into care without strain. The closing section brings the meditation into a larger view, suggesting that the sacred does not need to be manufactured and can be recognized in ordinary life.
Chapters: 01:24 Beginning with goodness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17637/?t=84 04:38 Settling in the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17637/?t=278 10:54 Breath as nourishment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17637/?t=654 18:09 Relaxing vigilance - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17637/?t=1089 19:55 Devoting ourselves here - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17637/?t=1195 27:33 The sacred already present - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17637/?t=1653

item.title: Guided Meditation: Letting the Breath Come to You
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation that begins by noticing what happens when we "sit together" and then shifts toward resting in what cannot be stopped. He gradually distinguishes the breath as a gentle object of attention while also emphasizing a broader openness in which awareness already receives everything. The practice returns again and again to the breath as something ordinary, nourishing, and not separate from the rest of life.

item.itunes:summary:

He gradually distinguishes the breath as a gentle object of attention while also emphasizing a broader openness in which awareness already receives everything. The practice returns again and again to the breath as something ordinary, nourishing, and not separate from the rest of life.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Self and Space
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation built around non-striving, resting in awareness, and easing the habit of making something out of the moment. He gradually moves from the simplicity of being alive, through repeated rests and reflections on self-making, toward a practice of leaning into what does not depend on the self. The talk closes by widening this into a daily practice of sincerity and asking, moment by moment, what opens space now.
Chapters: 00:00 Simple aliveness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17174/?t=0 02:57 Giving up acquisition - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17174/?t=177 07:49 Rest where awareness stays - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17174/?t=469 15:59 Putting down life - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17174/?t=959 20:11 Hindrances and disenchantment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17174/?t=1211 24:27 How self is reiterated - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17174/?t=1467 29:26 Rest in dependence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17174/?t=1766 34:15 Love and surrender - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17174/?t=2055 42:07 Practice in daily life - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17174/?t=2527
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Affect and Motivation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with posture, breath, and a felt sense of the body as a constellation of sensations rather than a fixed outline. He then turns toward affect and motivation, encouraging close attention to the body’s signals of wanting, aversion, and urgency without preferring spaciousness over solidification. In the later teaching, he links clinging to these affective circuits, points to how narratives try to explain experience, and returns to the value of staying with practice, equanimity, and the pace of awareness.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling into posture - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17161/?t=0 02:30 Breath and relaxation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17161/?t=150 03:58 Body as sensation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17161/?t=238 08:51 Softening the heart - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17161/?t=531 12:29 No preference for spaciousness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17161/?t=749 20:57 Affect and motivation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17161/?t=1257 26:52 Knowing and letting go - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17161/?t=1612 35:31 Clinging and narrative - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17161/?t=2131 46:23 Practice rhythms and closing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-17161/?t=2783
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Attention & Awareness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation that moves from settling into practice to a close look at attention, clinging, and the ease that comes from making peace with the present moment. He distinguishes the “spotlight” of attention from awareness itself, then gradually shifts toward resting in the knowing that cannot be turned off. The talk is quiet and reflective, with extended meditation space and a final emphasis on relinquishing control.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Sound, Outer and Inner
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on listening, beginning with settling and sensing goodness, then opening attention to the outer soundscape. The practice gradually turns toward sound as passing phenomenon, including the mind's own verbal thought, so that thinking can be heard more as another sound than as self. The talk stays close to direct experience and repeatedly returns to letting sounds move through without grasping.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Faith in Awareness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on trust in awareness rather than trying to engineer experience. The practice moves from settling posture and sensing what is present, to recognizing that even ordinary stillness is both familiar and fresh, to relaxing the search for signs of progress. He closes by emphasizing that the seeds of awakening are already here and that the practitioner’s work can become simpler: listen carefully, attune, and rest.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Unseduced by the Mirage of the Future
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a gentle guided meditation centered on loosening the habit of performing in practice and meeting experience without trying to prove anything. The meditation moves from settling the body and attention, to relaxing self-conscious effort, to seeing how the future can seduce the mind into anxiety and projection. He returns again and again to simple phrases of enoughness, encouraging a willingness to rest in what is already present.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation centered on letting attention settle into soothing phenomena and the silence behind experience. The practice moves from posture and settling, to finding ease in the body and environment, to resting with whatever arises without needing to fix anything. He closes by reframing practice as a simple Dharma gesture: let time pass, let the teachings unfold, and trust the wholesomeness of intention.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that moves from settling the body into a spacious way of meeting sound, sensation, and emotion. The talk gently reframes bracing and aversion as habits of protection, then invites a more trusting openness in which experience can arise without being organized around a defended center. In the second half, the practice softens toward a sustained sense of awareness as something that can hold and mellow the forces of suffering.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Effort and Effortlessness
item.description:
This guided meditation moves from settling the body into a simple, lightly held practice of attention. Matthew Brensilver emphasizes the difference between effortful attention and the more effortless quality of awareness, using the breath or another anchor as a relaxed point of return rather than something to force. Later, he invites a softer relationship to experience, including a memorable instruction to let the face fall off as a way of loosening identification and resting in openness.
Chapters: 02:54 Landing and settling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16824/?t=174 06:26 Choosing an anchor - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16824/?t=386 13:12 Effort and awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16824/?t=792 18:56 Relaxing the grip - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16824/?t=1136 20:17 Less mesmerized - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16824/?t=1217 25:01 Letting the face soften - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16824/?t=1501 27:38 Opening further - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16824/?t=1658
Source: AudioDharma

item.itunes:author: Matthew Brensilver
item.itunes:summary:
This guided meditation moves from settling the body into a simple, lightly held practice of attention. Matthew Brensilver emphasizes the difference between effortful attention and the more effortless quality of awareness, using the breath or another anchor as a relaxed point of return rather than something to force. Later, he invites a softer relationship to experience, including a memorable instruction to let the face fall off as a way of loosening identification and resting in openness.
Chapters: 02:54 Landing and settling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16824/?t=174 06:26 Choosing an anchor - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16824/?t=386 13:12 Effort and awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16824/?t=792 18:56 Relaxing the grip - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16824/?t=1136 20:17 Less mesmerized - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16824/?t=1217 25:01 Letting the face soften - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16824/?t=1501 27:38 Opening further - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16824/?t=1658
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/16824

item.title: Guided Meditation: Getting Somewhere by Going Nowhere
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores the paradox of meditating with aspiration while letting go of striving. He starts by contrasting goal-directed effort with a quieter, atelic way of practicing, then moves into a guided opening that settles attention into the body and softens grasping. From there the talk widens into a contemplation of awareness, liberation, and the way practice privileges presence over the relentless need to get somewhere.
Chapters: 00:27 Goals and Dukkha - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16821/?t=27 01:56 Telic and Atelic - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16821/?t=116 03:01 One Moment at a Time - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16821/?t=181 05:29 Aspiration Without Urgency - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16821/?t=329 08:38 Settling Into the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16821/?t=518 15:33 Liberation in Phenomena - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16821/?t=933 17:58 Relaxing the Struggle - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16821/?t=1078 23:29 Be Blissful - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16821/?t=1409
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Meditation as an Atelic Activity
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver begins with a philosopher’s critique of goal-directed living and connects it to the Buddha’s teaching on dukkha: the strain of always completing one thing in order to chase the next. He then turns that frame toward meditation, emphasizing that practice is not only a tool for improvement but also a chance to step out of striving and rest in present-moment experience. The guided portion keeps returning to the same point: the breath, the body, and even thinking itself are reminders to loosen achievement and let this moment be enough.
Chapters: 00:55 Goals and Their Limits - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16817/?t=55 02:22 Dukkha and Fatigue - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16817/?t=142 03:02 Meditation Beyond Goals - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16817/?t=182 04:21 Arriving in the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16817/?t=261 05:08 Settling and Surrendering - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16817/?t=308 11:39 When Striving Returns - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16817/?t=699 13:27 Breath as Reminder - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16817/?t=807 17:27 Thoughts Beside the Point - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16817/?t=1047 24:29 Rest in Imperfection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16817/?t=1469
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: No Fidelity to the Past Moment
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation built around the phrase “no fidelity to the past moment,” beginning with a short reading from Catherine Schultz about the mixed and changing nature of emotion. He then turns that passage into a Dharma point: practice does not try to preserve a mood or force a state to continue, and attention can stay open to both continuity and discontinuity. The sitting unfolds into instructions about landing in the body, relaxing the urge to narrate experience, and meeting whatever comes next with freshness and intimacy.
Chapters: 00:00 Opening Welcome - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16811/?t=0 00:44 Reading on Mixed Feeling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16811/?t=44 03:52 No Fidelity to the Past - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16811/?t=232 05:34 Settling Into Practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16811/?t=334 10:48 Listening With Spaciousness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16811/?t=648 13:58 Freshness and Discontinuity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16811/?t=838 25:22 Moment-by-Moment Offering - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16811/?t=1522
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Landing in the Body Breathing
item.description:
This guided meditation emphasizes landing attention in the body and breathing, then using love and awareness to meet whatever arises without turning experience into an enemy. Matthew Brensilver gently widens the practice from the body to the emotional patterns behind worry and the urge to fix and secure life, then returns again and again to the breath. The talk closes by framing mindfulness as a deep surrender that privileges awareness over projects.

item.title: Morning sit with instruction (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided morning sit centered on three facets of practice: attitude, attention, and awareness. The instruction moves from goodwill and bodily softening into several ways of placing attention—breath, whole body, visual field, and soundscape—while repeatedly returning to the easeful knowing that does not depend on effort. Later he turns to questions from retreatants, especially around pain, eating, and walking meditation, and emphasizes gentleness, imperfection, and the limits of control.

item.title: Guided Meditation
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with settling the body and attention, using Thich Nhat Hanh’s image of nectar sinking to the bottom of a glass to suggest a natural rather than forced calm. Matthew then moves between gentle instruction and long stretches of silence, first inviting a more precise focus and then a wider opening to whatever needs patience, love, and rest. In the closing section, he names the effort involved in directing attention and points toward an effortless dimension of awareness that does not need to be propped up.

item.title: Tuesday Sit with Instructions: Affect and Meaning-making
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet instruction-based sit centered on affect: how bodily feeling and the mind’s meaning-making shape one another. He repeatedly returns to softening the face, sensing urgency in the body, and meeting unpleasant feeling without turning it into a story about threat or failure. The talk gradually widens from simple relaxation instructions to a steadier, more ethical view in which equanimity, patience, and non-violence become easier to recognize.

item.title: Guided Meditation - Grief and Compassion (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on grief that treats sorrow as a natural part of loving in an impermanent world. The practice moves from settling the body into phrases of tenderness, mercy, and reverence, with brief references to Leonard Cohen, anicca, and Stephen Levine to steady the listener in uncertainty. The tone stays intimate and practical, emphasizing that compassion is not an extra but a necessity when loss keeps arriving.

item.title: Guided Meditation on Body and Affect (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with relaxing the face, shoulders, and belly, then widens into breath and whole-body awareness. From there, the talk turns toward affect: how feeling signals alarm, how vigilance and grasping show up in the body, and how equanimity can let experience remain without needing to fix it. In the second half, he shifts into retreat practice more broadly, especially the challenge of carrying mindfulness into walking, eating, and the ordinary moments where habits and self-consciousness most easily return.
Chapters: 00:01 Relaxing the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-70714/?t=1 05:14 Whole-body breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-70714/?t=314 10:31 Smoothing intensity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-70714/?t=631 15:44 Affect and alarm - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-70714/?t=944 18:56 Permission to settle - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-70714/?t=1136 22:02 Releasing grasping - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-70714/?t=1322 26:32 Continuity beyond the cushion - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-70714/?t=1592 41:33 Mindfulness and eating - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-70714/?t=2493 53:31 Retreat instructions - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-70714/?t=3211

From there, the talk turns toward affect: how feeling signals alarm, how vigilance and grasping show up in the body, and how equanimity can let experience remain without needing to fix it. In the second half, he shifts into retreat practice more broadly, especially the challenge of carrying mindfulness into walking, eating, and the ordinary moments where habits and self-consciousness most easily return.

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
This lightly guided meditation invites a gradual softening of vigilance, self-protection, and mental reference points. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to the felt sense of this moment, encouraging a relaxation into experience rather than a struggle to manage it. Midway through, he quotes a teacher’s instruction—“Don’t fight with yourself at any level”—and uses it to open into radical permission, intimacy, and equanimity.
Chapters: 00:57 Opening to Experience - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16271/?t=57 01:58 Safe Enough to Melt - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16271/?t=118 02:42 Letting Armor Unwind - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16271/?t=162 06:02 Dropping Reference Points - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16271/?t=362 10:00 No Fighting - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16271/?t=600 15:50 Tolerating Not Knowing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16271/?t=950 20:30 Resting the Heart - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16271/?t=1230
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Non-possessiveness and Joy
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation on non-possessiveness and sympathetic joy, beginning with settling the body and softening the mind’s need to sort every problem at once. The practice moves from releasing hope and fear into letting go, then into mudita: becoming porous to the happiness of others without comparison or threat. The closing invites an expansive wish for joy to increase for all beings, including those not yet born.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Compassion
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on compassion that begins with making space for the full range of human joy and sorrow, then settles into a steadier confidence in taking one’s seat amid ordinary imperfection. He gradually turns toward the suffering of someone cared about, first allowing grief to be felt and then shifting into warmth, blessing, and the wish that suffering be eased. The meditation closes by loosening the boundary between self and other and resting in a more open, distinction-less compassion.
Chapters: 00:12 Arriving and settling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16258/?t=12 01:25 Space for human life - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16258/?t=85 05:24 Relaxing control - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16258/?t=324 08:24 Blessing the moment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16258/?t=504 14:26 Seeing another's pain - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16258/?t=866 18:39 From grief to warmth - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16258/?t=1119 24:01 Offering care - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16258/?t=1441 27:24 Open compassion - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16258/?t=1644

item.title: Guided Meditation: Lovingkindness Practice
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided lovingkindness practice that begins by clarifying what metta is and is not: not niceness, and not pretending. He then turns attention to the body as a place of real goodness and generosity, before moving into the core phrases of metta and the felt sense of letting go of self-patrolling, self-hatred, and vigilance. The practice ends by emphasizing that this kindness is not earned but simply offered.
Chapters: 01:08 Metta, Not Niceness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16267/?t=68 03:38 Kindness Toward the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16267/?t=218 06:40 Noticing Quiet Goodness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16267/?t=400 16:45 The Bell of Phrases - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16267/?t=1005 20:25 May I Be Happy - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16267/?t=1225 22:20 May I Be Safe - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16267/?t=1340 27:42 Unconditional Offering - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16267/?t=1662
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided meditation that moves from settling the body into awareness to exploring the sense that awareness is always already present. He emphasizes relaxing effort, noticing what cannot be stopped, and resting in the open space in which breath, sensation, and thought arise. Midway through, a repeated invocation of Shantideva supports a spacious turning: instead of strategizing or fretting, one keeps folding life back into awareness.

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quietly guided meditation on softening into experience rather than trying to produce a special state. He returns repeatedly to the invitation to make the heart available, and links that availability with gratitude, non-moralistic presence, and a widening sense of innocence. The talk moves from settling the body and breath into a gentler relationship with judgment, aversion, and self-worth, ending with a simple emphasis on love coloring everything.
Chapters: 00:30 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16102/?t=30 02:29 Becoming Available - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16102/?t=149 06:47 Each Moment as Gift - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16102/?t=407 11:23 Beyond Judgment - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16102/?t=683 19:13 Basic Innocence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16102/?t=1153 20:25 Releasing the Past - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16102/?t=1225 22:00 Awareness Holds It - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16102/?t=1320 24:56 Love Flavors Everything - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-16102/?t=1496
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided meditation centered on posture, breath, and a gradual softening of effort. He repeatedly returns to the sense that we are not responsible for manufacturing insight or concentration, only for sincerity, willingness, and trust in the Dharma. The talk then narrows into simpler and simpler contact with experience—breath, body, feeling, sound, silence, even darkness behind closed eyes—while emphasizing receptivity over control.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided sitting practice built around stopping, easing in, and noticing how attention can rest without bracing or grasping. He frames meditation as a willingness to tolerate the loose ends of life, then narrows to a simple circle of practice and the question of what falls inside or outside it. As the talk develops, he opens that frame back out: every sensation, even difficulty, can become a door to wisdom, love, and a steadier present.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided meditation centered on breathing softly with the body, especially the exhale, as a way to settle intensity without trying to solve life’s larger problems. The practice moves from posture and full breaths into a patient attention to belly, face, jaw, and a general smoothing of bodily density. In the latter part of the talk, he reflects briefly on how emotional activation can drive thought, then returns to extended silent practice with very occasional reminders.

Source: AudioDharma

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a lightly guided meditation centered on letting attention rest differently: not as a spotlight from behind the eyes, but as the body being pervaded by awareness. He moves through images of earth, vibration, and spaciousness, then gently points to how present-moment experience can loosen control and soften the heart. In the closing reflections, he emphasizes that practice does not remove life’s complexity, but can resolve suffering in this moment.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Lightly Guided Meditation
item.description:
This talk begins as a lightly guided meditation, using ambient sound, breath, and body awareness to settle the room. Matthew Brensilver then turns toward how the mind keeps wanting to optimize experience, and he gently explores the relief of not sorting every moment into pleasant and unpleasant. The long middle and ending section becomes a quiet, repetitive accompaniment that supports staying with simple presence, though parts of the transcript appear unclear.

item.title: Guided compassion practice: grieving as a factor of growth
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided compassion practice that moves from settling the body into a reflection on grief as part of spiritual growth. He frames grief not as a failure of practice, but as a natural companion to love, ethical maturity, and a deeper willingness to care. The meditation then expands from holding one suffering person to radiating compassion more broadly, with repeated phrases and a closing blessing for all beings.

item.title: Guided meditation: feeling as a prediction of risk
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with a long settling into the body, using the breath, posture, and soundscape as a stable anchor while gently releasing bracing. It then turns toward Matthew Brensilver’s central theme: feeling as a prediction of risk, including urgency, aversion, and bodily pain, and how equanimity can keep those signals from turning into extra struggle. The closing section opens into brief questions about walking meditation, then returns to the practical tone of staying close, sensitive, and steady in retreat.

item.title: Guided Metta practice
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided metta practice that begins with a quiet reflection on why lovingkindness matters, especially when the alternatives are hatred, apathy, or self-judgment. He then moves into a steady seated practice with phrases for self, other, and all beings, while emphasizing that metta can work as cultivation, concentration, and purification. The talk stays grounded in the ordinary conditions of human life, showing how tenderness can include whatever is present rather than wait for a perfect feeling state.
Chapters: 00:00 Why Metta Matters - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-69738/?t=0 04:53 Three Functions - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-69738/?t=293 09:13 Settling the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-69738/?t=553 13:41 Self-Compassion Phrases - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-69738/?t=821 19:48 Extending to Others - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-69738/?t=1188 24:53 Warmth and Care - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-69738/?t=1493 30:01 Pain as Practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-69738/?t=1801 34:01 All Beings at Ease - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-69738/?t=2041 39:01 Closing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-69738/?t=2341

item.title: Guided Meditation: Letting Go Cultivates Stability
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a gently guided sit centered on landing in the body, gathering attention with the breath, and loosening the habit of trying to improve the moment. The talk moves from settling and gratitude into a clear explanation of why letting go can stabilize attention rather than weaken it. It then opens into an extended period of loving-kindness phrases, ending with the suggestion to trust awareness instead of endlessly optimizing experience.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14661/?t=12 05:24 Breath as Home - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14661/?t=324 11:21 Letting Go of Better - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14661/?t=681 17:21 Truce with Imperfection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14661/?t=1041 23:40 Loving-Kindness Phrases - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14661/?t=1420 29:10 Trusting Awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14661/?t=1750
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/14661

item.title: Guided Meditation: Don't Let Greed And Aversion Lurk Behind the Technique
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with a caution: reaching for technique can sometimes be a subtle way of avoiding greed, aversion, or clinging already present. He then leads a breath practice that becomes increasingly precise and steady, while naming the pull of worry, strategizing, and affect beneath thinking. The talk returns again and again to making peace with whatever is already here so the breath can feel like shelter rather than escape.
Chapters: 00:45 Technique and avoidance - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14568/?t=45 02:14 A subtle warning - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14568/?t=134 04:00 Settling the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14568/?t=240 06:29 Precise attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14568/?t=389 11:28 When attention wanders - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14568/?t=688 18:42 Shelter and release - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14568/?t=1122
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/14568

item.title: Guided Meditation: Not Reiterating or Reimaging Self
item.description:
This guided meditation invites a quiet shift away from managing experience and toward simply resting in awareness. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to the theme of not optimizing the moment, not building out a self-story, and not rehearsing a body-image or room-image that reinforces separation. The practice moves from settling the body and setting intention into a softer surrender of the autobiographical mind, with attention to urgent energy, spaciousness, and a final emphasis on trusting awareness without figuring anything out.
Chapters: 00:37 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14570/?t=37 01:54 Awareness First - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14570/?t=114 05:21 Resting Without Agenda - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14570/?t=321 09:52 Softening Self-Image - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14570/?t=592 12:46 Autobiography in Awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14570/?t=766 18:28 Meeting Urgency - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14570/?t=1108 20:09 Space Moving Through - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14570/?t=1209 27:12 Nothing To Figure Out - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14570/?t=1632
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Clinging Complicates Everything
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation on how clinging tightens experience and makes the mind feel complicated. He begins with breath and body cues for smoothing out energy, then turns toward greed, aversion, and the ways practice can become simpler when there is less grasping. The later part of the talk points toward resting more fully in the moment, with a brief Ajahn Chah reference shaping the theme of simplicity and letting go.
Chapters: 00:12 Settling In - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14560/?t=12 02:54 Balancing Energy - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14560/?t=174 05:50 Breath Through the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14560/?t=350 09:27 The Cost of Clinging - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14560/?t=567 12:15 Equanimity and Ease - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14560/?t=735 14:13 Resting in Simplicity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14560/?t=853 22:12 Sacred Enough - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14560/?t=1332
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/14560

item.title: Guided Meditation: Sound and the Openness of Awareness
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with settling the body through softening and relaxation, then gradually turns toward sound as a way to steady attention. Matthew Brensilver invites listeners to notice not only the arising and ending of sound, but also the spacious, receptive quality of awareness that can hold it all. The practice moves from directed attention to a quieter resting in awareness itself.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Grounding and Opening
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with a brief framing of the week’s theme—bringing Dharma into interpersonal life—before moving into a long practice of settling, grounding, and opening. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to the body, gravity, the exhalation, and a softening of effort, then widens into an awareness that can hold whatever is known. The talk gradually turns toward relinquishing fixing and becoming, treating imperfection itself as part of the path toward peace.
Chapters: 00:12 Opening Context - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14556/?t=12 02:57 Settling Into Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14556/?t=177 05:47 Awareness and Softening - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14556/?t=347 10:02 Holding Imperfection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14556/?t=602 12:23 Returning to Ground - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14556/?t=743 18:43 Letting Dharma Unfold - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-14556/?t=1123
Source: AudioDharma
Original page: https://www.audiodharma.org/talks/14556

item.title: Guided Meditation: Resting in Goodness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation oriented toward resting in goodness: recalling the wholesome qualities already present in one’s life, feeling gratitude for the Dharma lineage, and settling attention into the soundscape. The practice then turns toward a more open field of sound, silence, and the ease of not clinging. Near the end, he emphasizes that the heart needs rest and that relaxing into our human imperfection is not indulgent, but necessary.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Being Changed by Experience Rather than Changing it
item.description:
This guided meditation centers on allowing experience to alter us rather than trying to manage or shape it. Matthew Brensilver begins with posture and relaxation, then gradually turns attention toward the felt sense of experience, the urge to push and pull, and the willingness to be impacted. The practice settles into a quiet instruction to stand down, feel through urges, and let the heart be softened by what is already here.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Softening to the Ungovernability of Experience
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on softening to the ungovernability of experience: meeting discomfort without needing to organize, fix, or sort it all out. The practice moves between settling the body, feeling the weight of gravity, and opening to the reality of dukkha with a steadier, more tender mind. He returns again and again to the possibility that equanimity is not cold distance, but a quiet route into love.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Between Suppression and Entanglement
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation that moves from settling the body into sensing companionship and refuge. He emphasizes softening self-conscious tension, resting in the goodwill of Sangha, Buddha, and Dharma, and letting awareness bless experience without forcing it. The practice then opens into a spacious release of struggle, ending with a middle way he names as neither suppression nor entanglement.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Not Fighting with Ourselves
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on equanimity as a kind of nonviolence toward experience. He frames the practice as not fighting with the nervous system, softening around collisions of pain and contraction, and making peace with the human condition rather than trying to force experience to be אחרת. The meditation gradually opens into a reflection that equanimity does not make life colder; it can make the heart more responsive to both suffering and joy.

item.title: Guided Meditation - changing conditions
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by settling the body and noticing how silence feels, with an emphasis on allowing experience rather than forcing a particular inner posture. It then opens into investigation of hope and fear, dukkha, and the way the mind resists impermanence, using images of water, river, and flow to point toward anicca. The closing movement is gentle and practical: loosening the story of self, resting in change, and recognizing how that can support a more open love.

item.title: Reflections and Guided Heart Practice
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver opens with a brief communal reflection on the brahma-viharas and then turns toward why loving-kindness matters in practice: not as a pleasant mood, but as a softening of the heart. He connects this to a Buddha story about the “abodes of Brahma,” then reads Raymond Carver’s “Late Fragment” to widen the question of what it means to feel beloved on the earth. The second half becomes a guided loving-kindness practice centered on receiving kindness, first from imagined benefactors and then, if it feels right, extending it outward.
Chapters: 00:01 Opening the Heart Practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-68409/?t=1 00:33 Community and Receiving - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-68409/?t=33 05:06 Kindness Over Attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-68409/?t=306 09:01 Brahma-viharas Explained - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-68409/?t=541 11:35 Freedom and Tenderness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-68409/?t=695 13:51 Raymond Carver's Question - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-68409/?t=831 16:29 Metta as Free Heart - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-68409/?t=989 20:03 Settling and Imagining - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-68409/?t=1203 24:31 Receiving Kindness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-68409/?t=1471

item.title: Guided Meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation framed by brief reflections on why practice can be simple, restful, and honest about suffering. The talk moves from settling into the body and breath into a wider instruction on renunciation, boredom, and continuity of awareness in movement and daily life. He repeatedly returns to non-striving: just practice, just arrive, and let mindfulness build its own momentum.

item.itunes:author: Matthew Brensilver
item.itunes:summary:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation framed by brief reflections on why practice can be simple, restful, and honest about suffering. The talk moves from settling into the body and breath into a wider instruction on renunciation, boredom, and continuity of awareness in movement and daily life. He repeatedly returns to non-striving: just practice, just arrive, and let mindfulness build its own momentum.

Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Friday Morning Instructions: The non-compulsion of awareness
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet instruction on how awareness can hold experience without being driven by it. He moves from settling into posture and breath, to the way habit and memory color the present, to a more somatic recognition of clinging as pressure, urgency, and drivenness in the body. The talk then widens into a gentle reframe of liberation, ending with a reminder that the Dharma is flexible and that practice is about noticing what is skillful now.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Wednesday Morning Instructions: Clarifying Body
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided body meditation that begins with settling into surrounding silence and then turns toward gravity, exhalation, and the changing texture of sensation. The talk gradually reframes the body as a shifting field rather than a solid object, encouraging a truce with imperfection, a softer relationship to wanting to fix experience, and a steadier equanimity. In the closing minutes, he narrows the instruction to “protecting nothing” and carrying a thread of Dharma into the day through non-distractedness, tranquility, or mindfulness.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling Into Silence - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12545/?t=0 03:59 Gravity and Exhalation - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12545/?t=239 09:07 Whole-Body Awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12545/?t=547 11:44 Unifying Attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12545/?t=704 15:14 Clarifying Body Experience - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12545/?t=914 26:05 Truce With Imperfection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12545/?t=1565 36:14 Dharma Mind - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12545/?t=2174 40:25 Protecting Nothing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12545/?t=2425 48:14 Carrying Practice Forward - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12545/?t=2894

item.title: Monday Morning instructions
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver opens this retreat-day talk by reflecting on what meditation is for: rest, refuge, insight, steadiness, renunciation, and a more honest relationship to suffering and freedom. He then shifts into practical instruction for sitting and walking practice, emphasizing softness, continuity, patience, and a gentle willingness to meet boredom, clinging, and the changing conditions of the mind without forcing outcomes. The talk closes with encouragement to treat each moment of mindfulness as the next support for practice, rather than judging the whole day by how it feels.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Solid, Liquid, Gas
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on meeting experience without agenda, beginning with a gentle invitation to welcome what is here and to put down the burden of self-improvement. The practice then moves into an embodied reflection on impermanence, using the language of solid, liquid, and gas to show how suffering, craving, breath, body, and self-view continually change. In the final movement, the talk opens toward compassion, suggesting a tender way of meeting what feels heavy rather than trying to force it open.
Chapters: 00:00 Arriving Without Agenda - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12263/?t=0 02:23 Putting Down the Story - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12263/?t=143 05:30 Allowing What Remains - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12263/?t=330 11:13 Solid, Liquid, Gas - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12263/?t=673 16:25 Anicca in the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12263/?t=985 27:12 Nothing to Hold - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12263/?t=1632 36:57 Compassion for the Heavy - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12263/?t=2217 38:51 Closing Bells - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12263/?t=2331

item.title: Guided Meditation: Radical Permission to Arrive
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on "radical permission": beginning by settling attention in the room and in the body, then widening into the idea that nothing in experience needs to be excluded. The instruction moves from breath and posture into a spacious acceptance of thought, feeling, and sensation, before returning to practical guidance for walking meditation and continuity of mindfulness through the day. The tone is gentle and steady, with repeated emphasis on allowing what is here and trusting that practice can be both simple and complete.
Chapters: 00:01 Arrive in Space - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12257/?t=1 02:26 Nothing Excluded - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12257/?t=146 05:21 Permission to Be Here - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12257/?t=321 11:50 Radical Self-Acceptance - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12257/?t=710 21:22 Breath Trains the Mind - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12257/?t=1282 40:21 Gently with the First Day - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12257/?t=2421 47:24 Walking as Continuity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-12257/?t=2844

item.title: Guided Reflection & Meditation: Grief & Compassion
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet reflection on grief as part of loving well, moving from metta into compassion, impermanence, and the moral work of not hardening around pain. He speaks personally and also broadly about social suffering, then grounds the talk in a guided phrase practice and a reading of Hala Alyan’s poem "Spoiler." The ending returns to letting go as a form of freedom, not denial.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Feelings and their elaborations
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation focused on feeling tone: pleasant, unpleasant, and neutral, and how quickly those tones can turn into story, aversion, and identity. The guidance stays close to direct bodily experience, repeatedly returning attention to breath, posture, sensation, and the simple fact of what is present. He closes with a brief Rumi quote and a short Damasio passage that reinforce the teaching that feeling accompanies mind throughout ordinary experience.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Settling into the Body
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that moves gently from settling the body to trusting the breath as a stable cue. He emphasizes that resting is not negligence, but a way of loosening compulsive worry without losing care or ethics. The practice widens into a shared retreat intention, a softening into gravity and nature, and a patient trust in the Dharma’s logic.

item.title: Day 6 Morning Guided Instructions: Pervasiveness of Feeling (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
In this guided morning instruction, Matthew Brensilver points the listener toward a quieter allegiance to awareness than to the ordinary pull of life. He builds the practice around feeling: how it shapes thought, how it generates suffering and motivation, and how meeting it directly can soften rather than harden the heart. The talk moves from settling the body and grounding in gravity, into a close investigation of pleasant and unpleasant feeling, and then into a steadier confidence in non-abiding and radical permission.
Chapters: 00:00 Choosing Awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=0 02:52 Taking The Seat - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=172 08:10 Why Investigate - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=490 12:04 The Power Of Feeling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=724 15:04 Ajahn Chah's Instruction - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=904 21:23 Finding Feelings In The Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=1283 26:58 Thought And Cascade - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=1618 34:07 Blooming And Collapsing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=2047 38:57 Radical Permission - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=2337

item.itunes:author: Matthew Brensilver
item.itunes:summary:
In this guided morning instruction, Matthew Brensilver points the listener toward a quieter allegiance to awareness than to the ordinary pull of life. He builds the practice around feeling: how it shapes thought, how it generates suffering and motivation, and how meeting it directly can soften rather than harden the heart. The talk moves from settling the body and grounding in gravity, into a close investigation of pleasant and unpleasant feeling, and then into a steadier confidence in non-abiding and radical permission.
Chapters: 00:00 Choosing Awareness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=0 02:52 Taking The Seat - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=172 08:10 Why Investigate - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=490 12:04 The Power Of Feeling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=724 15:04 Ajahn Chah's Instruction - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=904 21:23 Finding Feelings In The Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=1283 26:58 Thought And Cascade - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=1618 34:07 Blooming And Collapsing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=2047 38:57 Radical Permission - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-59145/?t=2337
Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Day 2 Morning Guided Instructions (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This guided morning sit invites rest as something available even when experience feels imperfect. Matthew Brensilver moves from relaxing the body and settling into sensations, to tracking the breath with steadiness, and then into a broader trust in the Dharma’s timing and unfolding. The closing section turns practical, with retreat logistics and a brief emphasis on affinity groups and shared practice support.

item.title: Morning Instructions
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a grounded morning instruction centered on paying attention to life as it is, with care rather than ambition. He moves from meeting hindrances and “Mara” directly, to sensing the body as a changing field, to the Buddha’s teaching on conditionality as the basis for a more natural love. The talk closes by noticing subtle efforting in meditation and returning to curiosity without agenda.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Body and Space (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
A mostly guided meditation that begins with settling into the breath and a soft body scan, then gradually widens into mindfulness of sound, space, and the changing texture of experience. Matthew Brensilver emphasizes ease, honesty, and not pretending, suggesting that mindfulness makes room for what is present rather than pushing it away. The close turns toward impermanence: sensations, self, and even identity as process rather than fixed things.

item.title: Guided Meditation: How the world impacts the emotional body
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with a brief reflection on how the body carries emotional response, then moves into a long period of practice centered on breath, hands, and the felt sense of emotion. He emphasizes that sound, memory, pain, irritation, grief, and restlessness all register in the emotional body, and that meeting those sensations with steady awareness can soften the heart and make mindfulness a refuge. The talk closes by widening the teaching into daily life, including urgency, self-judgment, and the idea that practice unfolds in many chapters rather than one fixed method.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Body Settling, Pleasure of the Breath
item.description:
This guided meditation begins by meeting the fear and uncertainty that can arise at the start of retreat, and gently reframes practice as a movement toward love and safety. Matthew then leads a body-based settling, balancing alertness with relaxation, and gradually narrows attention to the breath. The talk emphasizes subtle pleasure in breathing, the ways thought pulls attention away, and the invitation to let go without forcing anything.
Chapters: 00:00 Meeting Fear with Love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-56638/?t=0 03:28 Posture and Grounding - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-56638/?t=208 04:42 Warming the Body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-56638/?t=282 09:08 Finding the Breath - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-56638/?t=548 11:40 Sustaining Contact - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-56638/?t=700 14:21 Pleasure in Breathing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-56638/?t=861 20:58 Thought and Letting Go - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-56638/?t=1258 22:44 Simple Return - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-56638/?t=1364 29:11 After the Bell - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-56638/?t=1751

item.title: Guided Meditation: The Ground of Loving Acceptance
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation on how the sense of self keeps seeking ground through activity, and how that search can soften when met with loving acceptance. The practice moves from noticing strain and self-fixing, to resting in breath and body, to working directly with feeling tone and the emotional body as the place where clinging gathers. Near the end, he turns toward ease, non-striving, and a quieter relationship with whatever is present, closing with a Mary Oliver line about letting the body love what it loves.
Chapters: 00:01 Selfing and strain - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-10078/?t=1 02:11 Loving acceptance begins - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-10078/?t=131 03:05 Finding the ground - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-10078/?t=185 06:28 No pressure to be someone - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-10078/?t=388 14:14 Feeling tone and clinging - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-10078/?t=854 18:39 Breathing into feeling - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-10078/?t=1119 25:17 Softening the body - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-10078/?t=1517 34:55 Ease and closing - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-10078/?t=2095
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Settling through Love, Deepening through Sound
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver guides a quiet meditation that begins with reflection on love as the force that holds life together and gradually turns toward felt experience, sound, and awareness. The practice moves from softening the heart to opening the field of listening, including the inner sound and the way thoughts can be heard as sound. In the final section, he returns to refuge, self-story, and a sustainable way of practicing without heroics.

Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Settling into the Pleasure of the Breath
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation that begins with settling the body and sensing the breath as a source of quiet pleasure. The practice then widens into a way of using that pleasure to signal safety, soften the heart, and stay with experience without sorting it into good and bad. In the latter part of the talk he turns to walking meditation, describing it as a bridge into daily life and a place to work gently with boredom, continuity, and attention.

item.title: guided meditation
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on settling the nervous system, stabilizing attention, and meeting the usual friction of practice with patience. He moves from posture and breathing instructions into ways of working with fatigue, agitation, and the tendency to make a meditation into a future project. The talk gradually widens into a gentle inquiry into self, fear, and the quiet that can come when attention is steady enough to rest.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the posture - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=0 01:28 Entering practice gently - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=88 05:36 Root and support - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=336 08:16 Breath and energy - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=496 11:53 Making peace with imperfection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=713 19:04 This life, not rehearsal - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=1144 23:36 Choosing the field of attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=1416 27:40 Steadying the inner gaze - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=1660 32:57 Scanning for clinging - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=1977

item.itunes:summary:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation centered on settling the nervous system, stabilizing attention, and meeting the usual friction of practice with patience. He moves from posture and breathing instructions into ways of working with fatigue, agitation, and the tendency to make a meditation into a future project. The talk gradually widens into a gentle inquiry into self, fear, and the quiet that can come when attention is steady enough to rest.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the posture - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=0 01:28 Entering practice gently - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=88 05:36 Root and support - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=336 08:16 Breath and energy - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=496 11:53 Making peace with imperfection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=713 19:04 This life, not rehearsal - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=1144 23:36 Choosing the field of attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=1416 27:40 Steadying the inner gaze - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=1660 32:57 Scanning for clinging - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-55368/?t=1977
Source: Dharma Seed
Original page: https://dharmaseed.org/talks/55368/

item.title: Sitting with Instructions (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver frames retreat practice as a way of fully digesting experience rather than adding something to a life already elsewhere. He moves from the ordinary senses and the mind’s movement, to love as the deeper measure of awakening, to the quiet freedom that comes when practice becomes inseparable from life. In the second half, he turns toward retreat ending: change, integration, tears, and the way a practice can continue to settle after the formal sitting is over.
Chapters: 05:01 Experience as life - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-53260/?t=301 08:57 Digesting the day - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-53260/?t=537 14:35 Love as measure - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-53260/?t=875 25:33 Awakening into love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-53260/?t=1533 34:27 Subtle freedom - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-53260/?t=2067 38:51 Endings and change - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-53260/?t=2331 43:12 Dreams and letting go - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-53260/?t=2592 48:08 Tears and integration - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-53260/?t=2888
Source: Dharma Seed

item.title: Day 6: Sitting with Instructions (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet instruction for sitting with honesty rather than performance. He moves from noticing urgency and defining the edge of attention, into a longer reflection on impermanence, where the present moment is not something to freeze but something to trust as it changes. The talk then turns toward mettā practice and closes with a practical reminder about continuity of awareness in ordinary life.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Turbulence and Peace (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver begins with a personal story about crying in a dark breathing group, using it to explore how grief, laughter, and a deep peace can coexist. He then moves into a practical account of mental noting, clinging, and the way practice reveals both the movement into selfing and the possibility of letting go. In the second half, he responds to questions about retreat energy, secular mindfulness, ethics, renunciation, and free will, ending with a quiet preference to keep trying without overclaiming certainty.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Clinging and Space (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This guided meditation begins with settling the body, scanning for how the retreat moment feels, and reassuring the mind that the conditions for freedom are already here. It then turns toward attention, letting go of what is not ours, and meeting craving, aversion, and imperfection with steadiness rather than resistance. In the later sections, Matthew opens out into compassion and nonviolence, drawing on Buddha teachings, Martin Luther King Jr., Shinzen Young, and the Dalai Lama to show how softening the heart can change how we meet experience and one another.

item.title: Talk: Guided Meditation: Insight Into Not-self
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver explores not-self as a lived practice rather than a philosophy, beginning with how we freeze loved ones, and ourselves, into familiar concepts. He moves through mental noting, the burden of selfing, and how clinging, fear, and self-protection make experience feel dense and brittle. The talk gradually opens into a calmer view in which impermanence loosens identification, and not-self becomes closely tied to love, freedom, and care for others.
Chapters: 00:00 Seeing Beyond Concepts - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8750/?t=0 03:36 Noting and Self Assumptions - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8750/?t=216 09:09 Not-Self as Practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8750/?t=549 13:39 Ego, Shame, and Comparison - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8750/?t=819 17:59 Curating the Self - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8750/?t=1079 24:57 Thought and the Sense of I - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8750/?t=1497 32:29 Narrative Gravity and Fear - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8750/?t=1949 41:37 Open Air of Change - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8750/?t=2497 46:53 Not-Self and Love - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8750/?t=2813

item.title: Guided Meditation: Letting Go of Becoming
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on how mindfulness can soften the urge to become someone, acquire something, or fix experience. The practice moves from simple questions about what it means to stop meditating or stop being still, into resting with safety, silence, and awareness, and then into a more pointed reflection on clinging to the future and the self. Later, the tone becomes more intimate and paradoxical, with reminders that suffering matters and doesn’t, that we can’t fill the heart’s hole from the outside, and that love remains after letting go.
Chapters: 00:00 Entering Stillness - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8747/?t=0 03:57 Safety and Simplicity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8747/?t=237 06:47 Awakening as Letting Go - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8747/?t=407 13:06 Space Through the Mind - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8747/?t=786 18:27 The Heart's Hole - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8747/?t=1107 24:24 Love After Letting Go - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8747/?t=1464 36:15 Closing Praise - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8747/?t=2175
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Guided Meditation: Emotions and Inner Safety
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on meeting emotion directly as bodily sensation, rather than turning it quickly into story, judgment, or strategy. He keeps returning to the theme of inner safety: staying close to the feeling body, softening around pain, and learning to receive pleasant and unpleasant states without clinging or bracing. Near the end, he broadens the practice into everyday life, using a brief story about a man living on the streets to show how "my problems" can look different when awareness is immediate and spacious.

item.title: Guided Meditation: Settling, Body, Breath
item.description:
Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation that moves from settling the body to resting with the breath, then into walking practice and the continuity of mindfulness through ordinary movement. He repeatedly invites a softer relationship to experience: relax the face and belly, let the body be dignified, and stop needing to sort every moment into right or wrong. The talk leans on practical instructions, with a few larger reflections on how practice can include boredom, uncertainty, and the mind’s habit of trying to protect itself.
Chapters: 00:00 Settling the mind - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8739/?t=0 03:22 Modest responsibilities - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8739/?t=202 04:47 Relax the face - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8739/?t=287 11:09 Upright and soft - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8739/?t=669 19:06 Making peace with imperfection - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8739/?t=1146 24:54 The breath at close range - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8739/?t=1494 40:04 Retreat as cooking - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8739/?t=2404 50:31 Walking and continuity - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/audiodharma-8739/?t=3031
Source: AudioDharma

item.title: Matthew Brensilver, Sylvia Boorstein: Day 2: Evening Practice Session (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This evening practice session opens with Sylvia Boorstein gently gathering the retreat together at the end of a few days of silence, then moves into a close reading of the Metta Sutta as both poetry and instruction. The talk lingers on how lovingkindness is not just a feeling but a disciplined way of seeing suffering clearly, withholding ill will, and training the mind through repetition. It ends by widening the circle to difficult people and to Martin Luther King Jr.’s language of loving enemies, linking compassion with freedom and steadiness.

item.title: Matthew Brensilver, Sylvia Boorstein: Day 2: Afternoon Practice Session (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
item.description:
This afternoon practice session moves from a simple story about family irritation into a wide-ranging reflection on attention, suffering, and the way practice changes how life is seen. Sylvia Boorstein uses stories from ordinary life, death, and retreat practice to show how clarity can cut through editorializing and reveal what is actually happening. The talk closes by returning to loving-kindness in movement, with walking practice turned toward wishing well for everyone encountered.
Chapters: 00:00 Practice as attention - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-48355/?t=0 08:36 Widening the field - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-48355/?t=516 22:57 Retreat and first tears - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-48355/?t=1377 44:51 Inching into Dharma - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-48355/?t=2691 55:22 Perspective and mortality - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-48355/?t=3322 01:06:56 Clarity and the heart - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-48355/?t=4016 01:18:57 Renunciation and dying - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-48355/?t=4737 01:29:55 The path in practice - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-48355/?t=5395 01:35:08 Walking with goodwill - https://jeffharr.is/brensilver/talks/dharmaseed-48355/?t=5708

item.title: Matthew Brensilver, Sylvia Boorstein: Day 2: Morning Practice Session
item.description:
This morning session begins with movement and posture instruction, then widens into a meditation on how the body can serve as a direct doorway into present-moment awareness. From there, the talk turns toward loving-kindness practice: simple phrases, the Metta Sutta, and the ways metta can include strangers, difficult people, and the whole world without forcing feeling. The closing stretch reflects on mindfulness, ethics, and whether insight is mature only when it also deepens decency, care, and responsiveness.
